如果 codenewbieteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 codenewbieteam：

降低 codenewbieteam 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 codenewbieteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hey there! Welcome to this week's edition of What I Learned. After a week of delving into new coding topics and reading your articles over on the #codenewbie tag, I'm excited to share some of my favorite finds with you.

If you aren't exploring or posting over on the #codenewbie tag, find the tag here!

Here's what I discovered this week (there has been no rhyme or reason this week, just a few scattered things I learned!):

  1. If you don’t know me, you should know that I REALLY love production line organization and documentation. I love to document different changes in my graphic design progress so I can feel my successes more and communicate with my team better. As I try to better understand our engineering team’s workflow, I have found articles like these pivotal in understanding documentation! If you need a Pull Request crash course, this is it.

@bekahhw states, “The great things about Pull Requests are that they allow for collaboration between contributors and maintainers, offer an opportunity to communicate changes that have been made and why they are important, and the ability for maintainers to provide feedback.”

@bekahhw also made this other article for @opensauced, further explaining Pull Requests!

  1. Spoiler: I am a huge fan of recommending that people do online courses and a huge hypocrite who has never done one (related to coding at least). But I can tell you for sure, articles like this one from @javinpaul help me motivate that part of my brain that I know will do one one day.
  1. When coming up with portfolio ideas in the past, I know I have sometimes pulled out my hair trying to come up with something with that good balance between sophistication, effortlessness, and uniqueness. If you need some more inspiration to get you through that hurdle, check out this article from @rammcodes

And— last but not least, if you haven’t engaged in any discussions today, here is one @ben posted today!

Thank you thank you for tuning in, have a great rest of your day y’all!

(PS: What random things are you learning this week???)

item.title: MS Clarity Video Recorder 非常棒（类似于 Google Analytics）并且适用于指南文档 | MS Clarity Video Recorder is amazing (something like Google Analytics) and suitable for Guide Documents
item.description:
我最近为自己的图书馆创建了一个指南文档主页，并将 Microsoft Clarity 附加到该主页。 几天后，我回到 Clarity，看到日志是用户操作的视频，质量给我留下了深刻的印象。

以前，当我开发一个开源库并创建一个指南文档时，我依赖于我周围的人的反馈。 听取周围人的抽象意见，我不得不决定人们更关注哪些内容，我应该在哪些地方写得更详细和强调。 有时我会通过 Google Analytics 查看访问者统计数据，但是有很多方面很难用简单的数字和图表来理解。

但是，在看了几个访问我主页的用户的动作日志视频后，我不再需要向周围的人征求意见。 这是因为我可以亲眼看到用户更关注哪些内容，哪些内容停留时间更长。

另外，就像上面的视频一样，我发现有人很长时间都在详细阅读我的手册。 因此，在编写指南文件时，比以前更仔细、更努力。

我不知道有与 MS Clarity 类似的服务。 但对于像我这样在业余时间创建开源库的人来说，这样的免费服务更具吸引力。

暂停后，samchon 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，samchon 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，samchon 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 samchon 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 samchon 的方法：

降低 samchon 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 samchon 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I recently created a homepage for guide documentation for my own library, and I attached Microsoft Clarity to that homepage. And after a few days, I went back to Clarity and saw the log as a video of user actions, and I was very impressed by the quality.

Previously, when I developed an open source library and created a guide document, I depended on people around me for feedback. Listening to the abstract opinions of the people around me, I had to decide which content people focused more on and where I should write in more detail and emphasize. Sometimes I looked at visitor statistics through Google Analytics, but there were many aspects that were difficult to understand with simple numbers and graphs.

However, after watching a few action log videos of users visiting my homepage, I no longer need to ask people around me for feedback. This is because I can see with my own eyes which content users pay more attention to and where they stay longer.

Also, like the video above, I found out that there are people who read my manual in detail for a long time. Therefore, in writing guide documents, more carefully and more effort than before.

I am not aware that there are similar services to MS Clarity. But for people like myself who create open source libraries in their spare time, such free service is more appealing.

manojradhakrishnan - Mar 23

Kuldeep Tarapara - Mar 23

Abishek Haththakage - Apr 9

item.title: 掌握竞争性编程的图算法：从基础到高级技术 | Mastering Graph Algorithms for Competitive Programming: From Basics to Advanced Techniques
item.description:
图算法是竞争性编程的基本工具。 它们提供了一种强大的方法来建模和解决广泛的现实世界问题，例如找到两点之间的最短路径、确定网络的连通性或识别社交网络中最有影响力的节点。 在此博客中，我们将探索各种图形算法，从基础到高级，并提供代码示例来说明它们的实现。

图是由边（或弧）连接的顶点（或节点）的集合。 在编程中表示图的常用方法有两种：邻接矩阵和邻接表。 邻接矩阵是一个二维数组，其中第 i 行第 j 列的条目表示顶点 i 和 j 之间的边权重。 另一方面，邻接表是一个列表数组，其中每个列表代表一个顶点的邻居。

代码示例（邻接矩阵）：

 // Graph representation using adjacency matrix const int MAXN = 100; // maximum number of vertices int graph[MAXN][MAXN]; // adjacency matrix // Initialize graph with all zeros memset(graph, 0, sizeof(graph));

代码示例（邻接表）：

 cppCopy code // Graph representation using adjacency list const int MAXN = 100; // maximum number of vertices vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list // Add an edge from u to v with weight w graph[u].push_back(v);

它使用一个队列来跟踪下一个要访问的顶点，并使用一个数组来标记访问过的顶点。 代码示例 (BFS)：

 cppCopy code // Breadth-First Search void bfs(int start, int end) { queue<int> q; // queue for BFS bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices int distance[MAXN] = {0}; // array to store distances from start q.push(start); visited[start] = true; while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); for (int v : graph[u]) { if (!visited[v]) { visited[v] = true; distance[v] = distance[u] + 1; q.push(v); } } } // Distance from start to end cout << "Distance from " << start << " to " << end << ": " << distance[end] << endl; }

它使用堆栈来跟踪下一个要访问的顶点，并使用数组来标记访问过的顶点。 代码示例（DFS）：

 cppCopy code // Depth-First Search void dfs(int u) { bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices // Recursive DFS void dfsUtil(int u) { visited[u] = true; cout << u << " "; // process vertex u for (int v : graph[u]) { if (!visited[v]) { dfsUtil(v); } } } dfsUtil(u); }

它使用优先级队列选择下一个距源点距离最小的顶点，并相应地更新其邻居的距离。 代码示例（Dijkstra 算法）：

 // Dijkstra's Algorithm void dijkstra(int start) { priority_queue<pair<int, int>, vector<pair<int, int>>, greater<pair<int, int>>> pq; // priority queue for Dijkstra's algorithm int distance[MAXN]; // array to store distances from start bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices // Initialize distances to infinity for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { distance[i] = INT_MAX; } distance[start] = 0; // distance from start to itself is 0 pq.push({0, start}); while (!pq.empty()) { int u = pq.top().second; pq.pop(); if (visited[u]) { continue; } visited[u] = true; for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { distance[v] = distance[u] + weight; pq.push({distance[v], v}); } } } // Print distances from start to all vertices cout << "Distances from " << start << " to all vertices: "; for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { cout << "Vertex " << i << ": " << distance[i] << " "; } cout << endl; }

它迭代地松弛图中的边，直到找到从源顶点到所有其他顶点的最短路径，即使存在负权重边也是如此。 代码示例（Bellman-Ford 算法）：

 cppCopy code // Bellman-Ford Algorithm void bellmanFord(int start) { int distance[MAXN]; // array to store distances from start // Initialize distances to infinity for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { distance[i] = INT_MAX; } distance[start] = 0; // distance from start to itself is 0 // Relax all edges repeatedly for (int i = 0; i < MAXN - 1; i++) { for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] != INT_MAX && distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { distance[v] = distance[u] + weight; } } } } // Check for negative cycles bool hasNegativeCycle = false; for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] != INT_MAX && distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { hasNegativeCycle = true; break; } } if (hasNegativeCycle) { cout << "Graph contains negative cycle!" << endl; } else { // Print distances from start to all vertices cout << "Distances from " << start << " to all vertices: "; for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { cout << "Vertex " << i << ": " << distance[i] << " "; } cout << endl; } } }

拓扑排序是一种算法，用于对有向无环图 (DAG) 的顶点进行线性排序，使得对于每个有向边 (u, v)，顶点 u 在排序中位于顶点 v 之前。 拓扑排序通常用于需要按特定顺序执行具有依赖关系的任务的调度问题。

代码示例（拓扑排序）：

 // Topological Sort vector<int> topologicalSort() { vector<int> result; // vector to store the topological order int indegree[MAXN] = {0}; // array to store indegree of each vertex // Compute indegree for each vertex for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto v : graph[u]) { indegree[v.first]++; } } queue<int> q; // queue for topological sort for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { if (indegree[u] == 0) { q.push(u); // add vertices with indegree 0 to the queue } } while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); result.push_back(u); // add vertex to the topological order for (auto v : graph[u]) { indegree[v.first]--; if (indegree[v.first] == 0) { q.push(v.first); // add vertices with indegree 0 to the queue } } } return result; }

它从一个空的边集开始，并迭代地向该集中添加边，同时确保不形成循环。 代码示例（Kruskal 算法）：

 cppCopy code // Kruskal's Algorithm int parent[MAXN]; // array to store parent of each vertex // Find function for disjoint-set data structure int find(int u) { if (parent[u] == u) { return u; } return parent[u] = find(parent[u]); } // Union function for disjoint-set data structure void unionSet(int u, int v) { parent[find(u)] = find(v); } // Kruskal's algorithm to find minimum spanning tree vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> kruskal() { vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> result; // vector to store edges of MST vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> edges; // vector to store all edges // Initialize parent array for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { parent[u] = u; } // Convert graph to edges vector for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; edges.push_back(make_pair(weight, make_pair(u, v))); // add edge to edges vector } } // Sort edges in ascending order of weights sort(edges.begin(), edges.end()); for (auto edge : edges) { int weight = edge.first; int u = edge.second.first; int v = edge.second.second; if (find(u) != find(v)) { // If u and v are not in the same set, add edge to MST result.push_back(make_pair(weight, make_pair(u, v))); unionSet(u, v); // union u and v in disjoint-set data structure } } return result; }

它还可以检测图中的负循环。 代码示例（Floyd-Warshall 算法）：

 // Floyd-Warshall Algorithm int distance[MAXN][MAXN]; // 2D array to store shortest distances void floydWarshall() { // Initialize distance array with INF (infinite) for unreachable pairs for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v = 0; v < MAXN; v++) { if (u == v) { distance[u][v] = 0; } else { distance[u][v] = INF; } } } // Update distance array with weights of edges for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; distance[u][v] = weight; } } // Compute shortest distances using dynamic programming for (int k = 0; k < MAXN; k++) { for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v = 0; v < MAXN; v++) { // If vertex k is an intermediate vertex in shortest path from u to v if (distance[u][k] != INF && distance[k][v] != INF && distance[u][k] + distance[k][v] < distance[u][v]) { distance[u][v] = distance[u][k] + distance[k][v]; // update shortest distance } } } } }

拓扑排序是有向无环图 (DAG) 顶点的线性排序，这样对于每个有向边 (u, v)，顶点 u 在排序中位于顶点 v 之前。 拓扑排序用于安排具有依赖关系的任务，例如项目管理、具有先决条件的课程安排，以及确定构建系统中的编译顺序。

代码示例（拓扑排序）：

 // Topological Sort vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list representation of the graph int indegree[MAXN]; // array to store indegree of vertices vector<int> result; // vector to store topological sort result bool topologicalSort() { queue<int> q; // queue to store vertices with indegree 0 // Compute indegree of vertices for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v : graph[u]) { indegree[v]++; } } // Push vertices with indegree 0 to the queue for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { if (indegree[u] == 0) { q.push(u); } } while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); result.push_back(u); // add u to topological sort result // Update indegree of neighboring vertices of u for (int v : graph[u]) { indegree[v]--; if (indegree[v] == 0) { q.push(v); } } } return result.size() == MAXN; // return true if topological sort is possible }

最大流量问题是一个经典的优化问题，它涉及寻找可以通过流量网络从源顶点发送到汇点顶点的最大流量，受边的容量限制。 它使用增广路径的概念来寻找网络中的最大流量。

代码示例（Ford-Fulkerson 算法）：

 // Ford-Fulkerson Algorithm vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list representation of the graph int capacity[MAXN][MAXN]; // capacity of edges int flow[MAXN][MAXN]; // flow through edges bool visited[MAXN]; // array to mark visited vertices int dfs(int u, int minCapacity, int sink) { visited[u] = true; // mark u as visited if (u == sink) { // Reached sink, return minCapacity as augmenting path found return minCapacity; } // Iterate through neighboring vertices of u for (int v :graph[u]) { if (!visited[v] && capacity[u][v] > flow[u][v]) { // If the edge has remaining capacity and v is not visited int updatedFlow = dfs(v, min(minCapacity, capacity[u][v] - flow[u][v]), sink); if (updatedFlow > 0) { // If an augmenting path is found // Update the flow and reverse flow in the residual graph flow[u][v] += updatedFlow; flow[v][u] -= updatedFlow; return updatedFlow; } } } return 0; // If no augmenting path is found } int maxFlow(int source, int sink) { int totalFlow = 0; // variable to store total flow // While there is an augmenting path from source to sink while (true) { memset(visited, false, sizeof(visited)); // reset visited array int updatedFlow = dfs(source, INF, sink); // find augmenting path if (updatedFlow == 0) { // If no augmenting path is found, break the loop break; } totalFlow += updatedFlow; // update total flow } return totalFlow; // return maximum flow }

最小生成树 (MST) 是一棵以尽可能小的总边权重跨越连通的无向图中所有顶点的树。 求最小生成树的算法有Kruskal算法、Prim算法、Boruvka算法等几种。 这些算法可用于查找图中的最小生成树，这在网络设计、聚类和其他优化问题中通常很有用。

代码示例（Kruskal 算法）：

 // Kruskal's Algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> edges; // vector to store edges with their weights vector<pair<int, int>> mst; // vector to store edges in minimum spanning tree int parent[MAXN]; // array to store parent of each vertex int rank[MAXN]; // array to store rank of each vertex // Function to find the parent of a vertex using path compression int find(int u) { if (parent[u] != u) { parent[u] = find(parent[u]); } return parent[u]; } // Function to union two disjoint sets using rank heuristic void unionSet(int u, int v) { int rootU = find(u); int rootV = find(v); if (rootU == rootV) { // If u and v are already in the same set, return return; } if (rank[rootU] < rank[rootV]) { // If rank of rootU is smaller than rank of rootV, make rootU parent of rootV parent[rootU] = rootV; } else { // Otherwise, make rootV parent of rootU parent[rootV] = rootU; if (rank[rootU] == rank[rootV]) { // If rank of rootU is equal to rank of rootV, increment the rank rank[rootU]++; } } } // Function to find minimum spanning tree using Kruskal's Algorithm void kruskal(int n) { sort(edges.begin(), edges.end()); // sort edges by weight in ascending order for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { parent[u] = u; // initialize parent of each vertex as itself rank[u] = 0; // Iterate through all the edges in sorted order for (auto edge : edges) { // Check if u and v are in different connected components if (find(u) != find(v)) { // If they are not in the same set, add the edge to the minimum spanning tree mst.push_back({u, v}); unionSet(u, v); // Union the sets containing u and v } } }

旅行商问题是一个经典的优化问题，其目标是找到访问给定城市集并返回起始城市的最短路线。 这个问题已知是 NP-hard 问题，这意味着没有已知的多项式时间算法可以解决所有情况。 然而，有几种近似算法可以在合理的时间内找到接近最优的解决方案。

一种常见的 TSP 近似算法是 Held-Karp 算法，其时间复杂度为 O(2^n * n^2)，其中 n 是城市数。 以下是 C++ 中 Held-Karp 算法的示例实现：

 const int MAX_N = 20; // maximum number of cities int n; // number of cities int dist[MAX_N][MAX_N]; // distance matrix int tsp(int mask, int pos) { if (mask == (1 << n) - 1) { // All cities visited, return distance from current city to starting city return dist[pos][0]; } int ans = INT_MAX; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (!(mask & (1 << i))) { // If city i is not visited yet int new_mask = mask | (1 << i); // set i-th bit in mask to 1 int new_dist = dist[pos][i] + tsp(new_mask, i); ans = min(ans, new_dist); } } return ans; } int main() { // Assume the distances between cities are stored in the dist matrix // Call tsp function starting from city 0 with mask 1 (representing city 0 as visited) int shortest_path = tsp(1, 0); cout << "Shortest TSP path: " << shortest_path << endl; return 0; }

最小顶点覆盖是一个图问题，其目标是找到覆盖图中所有边的最小可能顶点集。 换句话说，我们需要找到最小的一组顶点，使得图中的每条边都与该集合中的至少一个顶点相关联。 已知此问题是 NP-hard 问题，但有几种有效的算法可以近似解决方案。

最小顶点覆盖的一种常见算法是 Konig 定理，它将问题简化为在二部图中找到最大基数匹配。 下面是 Konig 定理在 C++ 中的示例实现：

 const int MAX_V = 100; // maximum number of vertices int n; // number of vertices vector<int> graph[MAX_V]; // adjacency list of the graph int match[MAX_V]; // stores the matching bool visited[MAX_V]; // keeps track of visited vertices bool augment(int u) { if (visited[u]) return false; visited[u] = true; for (int v : graph[u]) { if (match[v] == -1 || augment(match[v])) { // If v is not matched or we can find an augmenting path from v match[v] = u; return true; } } return false; } int konigs_theorem() { // Initialize match array with -1 memset(match, -1, sizeof(match)); int matching_size = 0; // size of the matching // Iterate over all vertices on the left side of the bipartite graph for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { // Reset visited array for each iteration memset(visited, false, sizeof(visited)); // Find augmenting path from current vertex u if (augment(u)) { matching_size++; } } // Minimum vertex cover is the set of unmatched vertices on the left side // and matched vertices on the right side vector<int> vertex_cover; for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { if (match[u] == -1 || visited[u]) { // If vertex u is unmatched on the left side or visited on the right side vertex_cover.push_back(u); } } // Print the minimum vertex cover cout << "Minimum Vertex Cover: "; for (int u : vertex_cover) { cout << u << " "; } cout << endl; return matching_size; } int main() { // Assume the graph is stored in the adjacency list format // Call konigs_theorem function to find the minimum vertex cover int matching_size = konigs_theorem(); cout << "Size of Maximum Cardinality Matching: " << matching_size << endl; return 0; }

最大流量是一个经典的图问题，其目标是在边上有容量的有向图中找到可以从源节点推送到汇节点的最大流量。 使用 Ford-Fulkerson 算法可以有效地解决这个问题，该算法具有多种变体，例如 Edmonds-Karp、Dinic 和 Push-Relabel 算法。 这些算法具有不同的时间复杂度，范围从 O(V^2 * E) 到 O(V^2 * sqrt(E))，其中 V 是图中的顶点数，E 是图中的边数。

下面是 Edmonds-Karp 算法在 C++ 中的一个示例实现：

 const int MAX_V = 100; // maximum number of vertices const int INF = 1e9; // a large enough value to represent infinity int n, m; // number of vertices and edges vector<int> graph[MAX_V]; // adjacency list of the graph int capacity[MAX_V][MAX_V]; // capacity matrix int flow[MAX_V][MAX_V]; // flow matrix int parent[MAX_V]; // stores the parent of each vertex in the augmenting path int bfs(int source, int sink) { memset(parent, -1, sizeof(parent)); // reset parent array parent[source] = -2; // set source's parent as a special value queue<pair<int, int>> q; q.push({source, INF}); // push source node with infinite capacity while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front().first; int curr_flow = q.front().second; q.pop(); for (int v : graph[u]) { if (parent[v] == -1 && capacity[u][v] > flow[u][v]) { // If v is not visited and there is residual capacity from u to v parent[v] = u; // set u as v's parent int new_flow = min(curr_flow, capacity[u][v] - flow[u[v]); // update new_flow as the minimum of current flow and residual capacity if (v == sink) { // If we have reached the sink node, we have found an augmenting path return new_flow; } q.push({v, new_flow}); // push v with the new flow as capacity } } } return 0; // If no augmenting path is found, return 0 } int edmonds_karp(int source, int sink) { int max_flow = 0; // stores the maximum flow while (true) { int curr_flow = bfs(source, sink); // find augmenting path if (curr_flow == 0) { // If no augmenting path is found, we have reached the maximum flow break; } max_flow += curr_flow; // add current flow to the total flow // Update flow and capacity along the augmenting path int u = sink; while (u != source) { int v = parent[u]; // get parent of u in the augmenting path flow[v][u] += curr_flow; // update flow from v to u flow[u][v] -= curr_flow; // update flow from u to v (backwards edge) u = v; // set u to its parent for the next iteration } } return max_flow; }

笔记：

这些只是如何在 C++ 中实现这些算法的简单示例。 根据您的问题的具体要求和限制，您可能需要相应地修改和自定义这些实现。 同样重要的是要注意这些实现假设输入图已经以适当的格式给出（例如，邻接列表或邻接矩阵）。 根据具体用例，可能需要输入验证、错误处理和其他优化。

结论：

图算法在竞争性编程中是必不可少的，因为它们可以用来有效地解决各种各样的问题。 在这篇博客中，我们介绍了几种重要的图算法，包括广度优先搜索 (BFS)、深度优先搜索 (DFS)、Dijkstra 算法、Bellman-Ford 算法、Floyd-Warshall 算法、拓扑排序、连接点、桥、Strongly连通分量 (SCC)、最大流和最小生成树。 我们为每个算法提供了代码示例，并解释了它们的时间复杂度和用例。

通过掌握这些图算法并了解它们的应用，您可以显着提高您的竞争编程技能并解决涉及图的各种问题。 不断练习、试验和学习，以精通在竞争性编程中使用图算法！



原文:

Graph algorithms are a fundamental tool in competitive programming. They provide a powerful way to model and solve a wide range of real-world problems, such as finding the shortest path between two points, determining the connectivity of a network, or identifying the most influential nodes in a social network. In this blog, we will explore various graph algorithms, from basic to advanced, and provide code examples to illustrate their implementation.

1. Graph Representation:

A graph is a collection of vertices (or nodes) connected by edges (or arcs). There are two common ways to represent a graph in programming: adjacency matrix and adjacency list. The adjacency matrix is a two-dimensional array where the entry at the i-th row and j-th column represents the edge weight between vertices i and j. The adjacency list, on the other hand, is an array of lists, where each list represents the neighbors of a vertex.

Code Example (Adjacency Matrix):


// Graph representation using adjacency matrix
const int MAXN = 100; // maximum number of vertices
int graph[MAXN][MAXN]; // adjacency matrix // Initialize graph with all zeros
memset(graph, 0, sizeof(graph));

Code Example (Adjacency List):

cppCopy code
// Graph representation using adjacency list
const int MAXN = 100; // maximum number of vertices
vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list // Add an edge from u to v with weight w
graph[u].push_back(v);

2. Breadth-First Search (BFS):

BFS is a graph traversal algorithm that visits all the vertices at the same level before moving to the next level. It uses a queue to keep track of the vertices to visit next and uses an array to mark visited vertices. BFS can be used to find the shortest path between two vertices in an unweighted graph, or to check if a graph is connected.

Code Example (BFS):

cppCopy code
// Breadth-First Search
void bfs(int start, int end) { queue<int> q; // queue for BFS bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices int distance[MAXN] = {0}; // array to store distances from start q.push(start); visited[start] = true; while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); for (int v : graph[u]) { if (!visited[v]) { visited[v] = true; distance[v] = distance[u] + 1; q.push(v); } } } // Distance from start to end cout << "Distance from " << start << " to " << end << ": " << distance[end] << endl;
}

3. Depth-First Search (DFS):

DFS is another graph traversal algorithm that explores a graph by recursively visiting the vertices and backtracking when necessary. It uses a stack to keep track of the vertices to visit next and uses an array to mark visited vertices. DFS can be used to find connected components in a graph, detect cycles, or solve problems that require exploring all possible paths in a graph.

Code Example (DFS):

cppCopy code
// Depth-First Search
void dfs(int u) { bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices // Recursive DFS void dfsUtil(int u) { visited[u] = true; cout << u << " "; // process vertex u for (int v : graph[u]) { if (!visited[v]) { dfsUtil(v); } } } dfsUtil(u);
}

4. Dijkstra's Algorithm:

Dijkstra's algorithm is a popular shortest path algorithm that finds the shortest path from a source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph. It uses a priority queue to select the next vertex with the smallest distance from the source and updates the distances of its neighbors accordingly. Dijkstra's algorithm is widely used in routing and navigation applications.

Code Example (Dijkstra's Algorithm):


// Dijkstra's Algorithm
void dijkstra(int start) { priority_queue<pair<int, int>, vector<pair<int, int>>, greater<pair<int, int>>> pq; // priority queue for Dijkstra's algorithm int distance[MAXN]; // array to store distances from start bool visited[MAXN] = {false}; // array to mark visited vertices // Initialize distances to infinity for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { distance[i] = INT_MAX; } distance[start] = 0; // distance from start to itself is 0 pq.push({0, start}); while (!pq.empty()) { int u = pq.top().second; pq.pop(); if (visited[u]) { continue; } visited[u] = true; for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { distance[v] = distance[u] + weight; pq.push({distance[v], v}); } } } // Print distances from start to all vertices cout << "Distances from " << start << " to all vertices: "; for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { cout << "Vertex " << i << ": " << distance[i] << " "; } cout << endl;
}

5. Bellman-Ford Algorithm:

Bellman-Ford algorithm is another shortest path algorithm that can handle negative weight edges, unlike Dijkstra's algorithm. It iteratively relaxes the edges in the graph until it finds the shortest path from a source vertex to all other vertices, even in the presence of negative weight edges. Bellman-Ford algorithm is commonly used in scenarios where negative weights are allowed, such as in time-dependent routing problems.

Code Example (Bellman-Ford Algorithm):

cppCopy code
// Bellman-Ford Algorithm
void bellmanFord(int start) { int distance[MAXN]; // array to store distances from start // Initialize distances to infinity for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { distance[i] = INT_MAX; } distance[start] = 0; // distance from start to itself is 0 // Relax all edges repeatedly for (int i = 0; i < MAXN - 1; i++) { for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] != INT_MAX && distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { distance[v] = distance[u] + weight; } } } } // Check for negative cycles bool hasNegativeCycle = false; for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; if (distance[u] != INT_MAX && distance[u] + weight < distance[v]) { hasNegativeCycle = true; break; } } if (hasNegativeCycle) { cout << "Graph contains negative cycle!" << endl; } else { // Print distances from start to all vertices cout << "Distances from " << start << " to all vertices: "; for (int i = 0; i < MAXN; i++) { cout << "Vertex " << i << ": " << distance[i] << " "; } cout << endl; } }
}

6. Topological Sort:

Topological sort is an algorithm used to linearly order the vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge (u, v), vertex u comes before vertex v in the ordering. Topological sort is commonly used in scheduling problems where tasks with dependencies need to be executed in a specific order.

Code Example (Topological Sort):


// Topological Sort
vector<int> topologicalSort() { vector<int> result; // vector to store the topological order int indegree[MAXN] = {0}; // array to store indegree of each vertex // Compute indegree for each vertex for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto v : graph[u]) { indegree[v.first]++; } } queue<int> q; // queue for topological sort for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { if (indegree[u] == 0) { q.push(u); // add vertices with indegree 0 to the queue } } while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); result.push_back(u); // add vertex to the topological order for (auto v : graph[u]) { indegree[v.first]--; if (indegree[v.first] == 0) { q.push(v.first); // add vertices with indegree 0 to the queue } } } return result;
}

7. Kruskal's Algorithm:

Kruskal's algorithm is a popular algorithm used to find the minimum spanning tree (MST) of a connected, undirected graph. It starts with an empty set of edges and iteratively adds edges to the set while ensuring that no cycles are formed. Kruskal's algorithm is commonly used in network design and clustering applications.

Code Example (Kruskal's Algorithm):

cppCopy code
// Kruskal's Algorithm
int parent[MAXN]; // array to store parent of each vertex // Find function for disjoint-set data structure
int find(int u) { if (parent[u] == u) { return u; } return parent[u] = find(parent[u]);
} // Union function for disjoint-set data structure
void unionSet(int u, int v) { parent[find(u)] = find(v);
} // Kruskal's algorithm to find minimum spanning tree
vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> kruskal() { vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> result; // vector to store edges of MST vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> edges; // vector to store all edges // Initialize parent array for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { parent[u] = u; } // Convert graph to edges vector for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; edges.push_back(make_pair(weight, make_pair(u, v))); // add edge to edges vector } } // Sort edges in ascending order of weights sort(edges.begin(), edges.end()); for (auto edge : edges) { int weight = edge.first; int u = edge.second.first; int v = edge.second.second; if (find(u) != find(v)) { // If u and v are not in the same set, add edge to MST result.push_back(make_pair(weight, make_pair(u, v))); unionSet(u, v); // union u and v in disjoint-set data structure } } return result; }

8. Floyd-Warshall Algorithm:

The Floyd-Warshall algorithm is a dynamic programming algorithm used to find the shortest path between all pairs of vertices in a weighted directed graph. It can also detect negative cycles in the graph. The Floyd-Warshall algorithm is commonly used in routing algorithms, traffic flow analysis, and network planning.

Code Example (Floyd-Warshall Algorithm):


// Floyd-Warshall Algorithm
int distance[MAXN][MAXN]; // 2D array to store shortest distances void floydWarshall() { // Initialize distance array with INF (infinite) for unreachable pairs for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v = 0; v < MAXN; v++) { if (u == v) { distance[u][v] = 0; } else { distance[u][v] = INF; } } } // Update distance array with weights of edges for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (auto edge : graph[u]) { int v = edge.first; int weight = edge.second; distance[u][v] = weight; } } // Compute shortest distances using dynamic programming for (int k = 0; k < MAXN; k++) { for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v = 0; v < MAXN; v++) { // If vertex k is an intermediate vertex in shortest path from u to v if (distance[u][k] != INF && distance[k][v] != INF && distance[u][k] + distance[k][v] < distance[u][v]) { distance[u][v] = distance[u][k] + distance[k][v]; // update shortest distance } } } }
}

9. Topological Sort:

Topological sort is a linear ordering of the vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) such that for every directed edge (u, v), vertex u comes before vertex v in the ordering. Topological sort is used in scheduling tasks with dependencies, such as in project management, scheduling of courses with prerequisites, and determining the order of compilation in a build system.

Code Example (Topological Sort):


// Topological Sort
vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list representation of the graph
int indegree[MAXN]; // array to store indegree of vertices
vector<int> result; // vector to store topological sort result bool topologicalSort() { queue<int> q; // queue to store vertices with indegree 0 // Compute indegree of vertices for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { for (int v : graph[u]) { indegree[v]++; } } // Push vertices with indegree 0 to the queue for (int u = 0; u < MAXN; u++) { if (indegree[u] == 0) { q.push(u); } } while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front(); q.pop(); result.push_back(u); // add u to topological sort result // Update indegree of neighboring vertices of u for (int v : graph[u]) { indegree[v]--; if (indegree[v] == 0) { q.push(v); } } } return result.size() == MAXN; // return true if topological sort is possible
}

10. Maximum Flow (Ford-Fulkerson Algorithm):

The maximum flow problem is a classical optimization problem that involves finding the maximum amount of flow that can be sent through a flow network from a source vertex to a sink vertex, subject to capacity constraints on the edges. The Ford-Fulkerson algorithm is a popular algorithm used to solve the maximum flow problem. It uses the concept of augmenting paths to find the maximum flow in a network.

Code Example (Ford-Fulkerson Algorithm):


// Ford-Fulkerson Algorithm
vector<int> graph[MAXN]; // adjacency list representation of the graph
int capacity[MAXN][MAXN]; // capacity of edges
int flow[MAXN][MAXN]; // flow through edges
bool visited[MAXN]; // array to mark visited vertices int dfs(int u, int minCapacity, int sink) { visited[u] = true; // mark u as visited if (u == sink) { // Reached sink, return minCapacity as augmenting path found return minCapacity; } // Iterate through neighboring vertices of u for (int v :graph[u]) { if (!visited[v] && capacity[u][v] > flow[u][v]) { // If the edge has remaining capacity and v is not visited int updatedFlow = dfs(v, min(minCapacity, capacity[u][v] - flow[u][v]), sink); if (updatedFlow > 0) { // If an augmenting path is found // Update the flow and reverse flow in the residual graph flow[u][v] += updatedFlow; flow[v][u] -= updatedFlow; return updatedFlow; } } } return 0; // If no augmenting path is found
}
int maxFlow(int source, int sink) {
int totalFlow = 0; // variable to store total flow
// While there is an augmenting path from source to sink
while (true) { memset(visited, false, sizeof(visited)); // reset visited array int updatedFlow = dfs(source, INF, sink); // find augmenting path if (updatedFlow == 0) { // If no augmenting path is found, break the loop break; } totalFlow += updatedFlow; // update total flow
} return totalFlow; // return maximum flow
}

11. Minimum Spanning Tree:

A minimum spanning tree (MST) is a tree that spans all the vertices in a connected, undirected graph with the minimum possible total edge weight. There are several algorithms to find the minimum spanning tree, such as Kruskal's algorithm, Prim's algorithm, and Boruvka's algorithm. These algorithms can be used to find the minimum spanning tree in a graph, which is often useful in network design, clustering, and other optimization problems.

Code Example (Kruskal's Algorithm):


// Kruskal's Algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree
vector<pair<int, pair<int, int>>> edges; // vector to store edges with their weights
vector<pair<int, int>> mst; // vector to store edges in minimum spanning tree
int parent[MAXN]; // array to store parent of each vertex
int rank[MAXN]; // array to store rank of each vertex // Function to find the parent of a vertex using path compression
int find(int u) { if (parent[u] != u) { parent[u] = find(parent[u]); } return parent[u];
} // Function to union two disjoint sets using rank heuristic
void unionSet(int u, int v) { int rootU = find(u); int rootV = find(v); if (rootU == rootV) { // If u and v are already in the same set, return return; } if (rank[rootU] < rank[rootV]) { // If rank of rootU is smaller than rank of rootV, make rootU parent of rootV parent[rootU] = rootV; } else { // Otherwise, make rootV parent of rootU parent[rootV] = rootU; if (rank[rootU] == rank[rootV]) { // If rank of rootU is equal to rank of rootV, increment the rank rank[rootU]++; } }
} // Function to find minimum spanning tree using Kruskal's Algorithm
void kruskal(int n) { sort(edges.begin(), edges.end()); // sort edges by weight in ascending order for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { parent[u] = u; // initialize parent of each vertex as itself rank[u] = 0; // Iterate through all the edges in sorted order for (auto edge : edges) { // Check if u and v are in different connected components if (find(u) != find(v)) { // If they are not in the same set, add the edge to the minimum spanning tree mst.push_back({u, v}); unionSet(u, v); // Union the sets containing u and v } }
}

12. Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP):

The Travelling Salesman Problem is a classic optimization problem where the goal is to find the shortest possible route that visits a given set of cities and returns to the starting city. This problem is known to be NP-hard, meaning there is no known polynomial-time algorithm to solve it for all cases. However, there are several approximate algorithms that can find near-optimal solutions in reasonable time.

One common approximate algorithm for TSP is the Held-Karp algorithm, which has a time complexity of O(2^n * n^2), where n is the number of cities. The basic idea of the Held-Karp algorithm is to use dynamic programming to efficiently calculate the shortest path for subproblems, and then combine these subproblems to find the overall shortest path.

Here's an example implementation of the Held-Karp algorithm in C++:

const int MAX_N = 20; // maximum number of cities
int n; // number of cities
int dist[MAX_N][MAX_N]; // distance matrix int tsp(int mask, int pos)
{ if (mask == (1 << n) - 1) { // All cities visited, return distance from current city to starting city return dist[pos][0]; } int ans = INT_MAX; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (!(mask & (1 << i))) { // If city i is not visited yet int new_mask = mask | (1 << i); // set i-th bit in mask to 1 int new_dist = dist[pos][i] + tsp(new_mask, i); ans = min(ans, new_dist); } } return ans;
} int main()
{ // Assume the distances between cities are stored in the dist matrix // Call tsp function starting from city 0 with mask 1 (representing city 0 as visited) int shortest_path = tsp(1, 0); cout << "Shortest TSP path: " << shortest_path << endl; return 0;
}

13. Minimum Vertex Cover:

Minimum Vertex Cover is a graph problem where the goal is to find the smallest possible set of vertices that covers all the edges in a graph. In other words, we need to find the smallest set of vertices such that every edge in the graph is incident to at least one vertex in that set. This problem is known to be NP-hard, but there are several efficient algorithms to approximate the solution.

One common algorithm for Minimum Vertex Cover is the Konig's theorem, which reduces the problem to finding a maximum cardinality matching in a bipartite graph. The time complexity of Konig's theorem is O(V^3), where V is the number of vertices in the graph.

Here's an example implementation of Konig's theorem in C++:

const int MAX_V = 100; // maximum number of vertices
int n; // number of vertices
vector<int> graph[MAX_V]; // adjacency list of the graph
int match[MAX_V]; // stores the matching
bool visited[MAX_V]; // keeps track of visited vertices bool augment(int u)
{ if (visited[u]) return false; visited[u] = true; for (int v : graph[u]) { if (match[v] == -1 || augment(match[v])) { // If v is not matched or we can find an augmenting path from v match[v] = u; return true; } } return false;
} int konigs_theorem()
{ // Initialize match array with -1 memset(match, -1, sizeof(match)); int matching_size = 0; // size of the matching // Iterate over all vertices on the left side of the bipartite graph for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { // Reset visited array for each iteration memset(visited, false, sizeof(visited)); // Find augmenting path from current vertex u if (augment(u)) { matching_size++; } } // Minimum vertex cover is the set of unmatched vertices on the left side // and matched vertices on the right side vector<int> vertex_cover; for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) { if (match[u] == -1 || visited[u]) { // If vertex u is unmatched on the left side or visited on the right side vertex_cover.push_back(u); } } // Print the minimum vertex cover cout << "Minimum Vertex Cover: "; for (int u : vertex_cover) { cout << u << " "; } cout << endl; return matching_size;
} int main()
{ // Assume the graph is stored in the adjacency list format // Call konigs_theorem function to find the minimum vertex cover int matching_size = konigs_theorem(); cout << "Size of Maximum Cardinality Matching: " << matching_size << endl; return 0;
}

14. Maximum Flow (Edmonds-Karp algorithm):

Maximum Flow is a classical graph problem where the goal is to find the maximum amount of flow that can be pushed from a source node to a sink node in a directed graph with capacities on the edges. This problem can be efficiently solved using the Ford-Fulkerson algorithm, which has several variations such as Edmonds-Karp, Dinic, and Push-Relabel algorithms. These algorithms have different time complexities, ranging from O(V^2 * E) to O(V^2 * sqrt(E)), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges in the graph.

Here's an example implementation of the Edmonds-Karp algorithm in C++:

const int MAX_V = 100; // maximum number of vertices
const int INF = 1e9; // a large enough value to represent infinity
int n, m; // number of vertices and edges
vector<int> graph[MAX_V]; // adjacency list of the graph
int capacity[MAX_V][MAX_V]; // capacity matrix
int flow[MAX_V][MAX_V]; // flow matrix
int parent[MAX_V]; // stores the parent of each vertex in the augmenting path int bfs(int source, int sink)
{ memset(parent, -1, sizeof(parent)); // reset parent array parent[source] = -2; // set source's parent as a special value queue<pair<int, int>> q; q.push({source, INF}); // push source node with infinite capacity while (!q.empty()) { int u = q.front().first; int curr_flow = q.front().second; q.pop(); for (int v : graph[u]) { if (parent[v] == -1 && capacity[u][v] > flow[u][v]) { // If v is not visited and there is residual capacity from u to v parent[v] = u; // set u as v's parent int new_flow = min(curr_flow, capacity[u][v] - flow[u[v]); // update new_flow as the minimum of current flow and residual capacity if (v == sink) { // If we have reached the sink node, we have found an augmenting path return new_flow; } q.push({v, new_flow}); // push v with the new flow as capacity } } } return 0; // If no augmenting path is found, return 0
}
int edmonds_karp(int source, int sink)
{ int max_flow = 0; // stores the maximum flow while (true) { int curr_flow = bfs(source, sink); // find augmenting path if (curr_flow == 0) { // If no augmenting path is found, we have reached the maximum flow break; } max_flow += curr_flow; // add current flow to the total flow // Update flow and capacity along the augmenting path int u = sink; while (u != source) { int v = parent[u]; // get parent of u in the augmenting path flow[v][u] += curr_flow; // update flow from v to u flow[u][v] -= curr_flow; // update flow from u to v (backwards edge) u = v; // set u to its parent for the next iteration } } return max_flow;
}

Note:

These are just simple examples of how these algorithms can be implemented in C++. Depending on the specific requirements and constraints of your problem, you may need to modify and customize these implementations accordingly. It's also important to note that these implementations assume that the input graph is already given in the appropriate format (e.g., adjacency list or adjacency matrix). Input validation, error handling, and other optimizations may be necessary depending on the specific use case.

Conclusion:

Graph algorithms are essential in competitive programming as they can be used to solve a wide variety of problems efficiently. In this blog, we covered several important graph algorithms, including Breadth-First Search (BFS), Depth-First Search (DFS), Dijkstra's algorithm, Bellman-Ford algorithm, Floyd-Warshall algorithm, Topological Sort, Articulation Points, Bridges, Strongly Connected Components (SCC), Maximum Flow, and Minimum Spanning Tree. We provided code examples for each algorithm and explained their time complexity and use cases.

By mastering these graph algorithms and understanding their applications, you can significantly improve your competitive programming skills and tackle a wide range of problems that involve graphs. Keep practicing, experimenting, and learning to become proficient in using graph algorithms in competitive programming!
item.author: rishitashaw

item.title: 2023 年将免费提供 7 款时间跟踪应用 | 7 Time Tracking Apps Available for Free in 2023
item.description:
如果您不熟悉员工时间跟踪或者直到现在一直在手动跟踪时间，那么使用免费时间跟踪应用程序可能是了解这些应用程序功能的一种经济高效的方式。 然而，市场上有大量的时间跟踪应用程序，每个应用程序都有不同的功能和价格，选择合适的解决方案可能会让人望而生畏。 为了帮助做出这一决定，我们确定了七款最适合小型企业的时间跟踪应用程序。

为了帮助您寻找理想的时间跟踪应用程序，我们精选了 2023 年最热门的免费时间跟踪应用程序合集。

我的时间：最适合自由职业者
价格范围：30 天免费（无需信用卡）每位用户每月 10 美元

用户限制：无限制

支持的平台：
适用于 Edge、Chrome 和 Firefox 的网络应用程序； 适用于 Linux、Mac 和 Windows 的桌面应用程序； 适用于 iOS 和 Android 的移动应用程序。

我们为什么选择它：

由于其全面的基于云的时间跟踪功能，JCards 被选为最佳整体时间跟踪应用程序。 使用 JCards，用户可以轻松跟踪各种项目、客户和设备上的时间。 该应用程序还允许进行出勤跟踪、薪资和会计优化、计费时间和费用跟踪，以及与客户共享项目进度的能力。

这些计划提供额外的功能，例如为他人增加时间、执行时间审计、包括员工休息时间等。

谁应该使用它：

优点：

  • 直观的时间表，
  • 逾期任务提醒，
  • 离线计时，
  • 综合日历，
  • 项目跟踪，
  • 深入的报告和过滤，
  • 开具发票，
  • 费用记录，
  • 时间表批准，
  • 休息时间跟踪。

缺点：

虽然 JCards 提供了一套全面的功能，但一些用户可能会发现该应用程序缺少高级自定义选项。 此外，由于 JCards 主要侧重于时间跟踪和项目管理，它可能不适合具有更复杂需求的企业，例如需要广泛的客户关系管理或会计功能的企业。

时光营：

优点：非常适合具有高级监控和生产力功能的远程团队自动时间跟踪和时间表批准

可定制的计费率和发票

缺点：对于某些用户来说，用户界面可能会让人不知所措某些功能可能需要与其他应用程序或服务进行额外集成

与网络应用程序相比，移动应用程序可能缺少一些功能

切换轨道：

优点：

简单直观的界面非常易于使用非常适合非常小的团队或需求有限的自由职业者

与广泛的应用程序和服务集成

缺点：

可能缺少大型企业或团队所需的一些高级功能免费版本中的发票功能有限

一些用户可能更喜欢更详细和更强大的报告系统

支付宝：

优点：

出色的发票和项目管理功能包括各种用于团队协作和沟通的工具

具有各种项目指标的可定制仪表板

缺点：

比此列表中的其他选项更昂贵，尤其是对于较大的团队一些用户可能会发现界面混乱或难以导航

较低的定价层可能缺少某些功能

我的时间：

优点：

非常适合具有简单时间跟踪需求的自由职业者可定制的项目和任务跟踪

自动发票和报告功能

缺点：

可能缺乏大型团队或企业所需的高级功能一些用户可能会觉得界面过于简单或缺乏视觉吸引力

与其他应用程序和服务的集成可能会受到限制

副：

优点：

卓越的日程安排和员工管理功能可定制的时间表和自动工资计算

包括一系列用于团队沟通和任务管理的工具

缺点：

比此列表中的其他一些选项更昂贵，尤其是对于较大的团队某些功能可能需要额外的设置或配置

一些用户可能会发现界面复杂或难以导航

追踪时间：

优点：

因其视觉时间跟踪功能而受到青睐包括一系列用于团队协作和项目管理的工具

可定制的报告和发票选项

缺点：

可能缺少大型团队或企业所需的一些高级功能一些用户可能会发现界面过于简单或缺少某些功能

免费版可能会限制某些功能

如何选择空闲时间跟踪应用程序

考虑到市场上有许多类似的应用程序，选择正确的时间跟踪应用程序可能会令人沮丧。 以下是一些指导您进行搜索的指南。 根据以下参数评估您正在考虑的所有应用程序，以选择最适合您团队的应用程序。

易用性：评估该应用程序的易用性，以确保该应用程序在您的组织中得到广泛采用并取得成功。

时间跟踪和报告：确保应用程序可以跟踪不同的参数，包括上班/下班时间、计费和非计费时间，并提供有关这些参数的可靠报告，然后可用于评估员工生产力和影响力。

团队管理功能：检查该应用程序是否允许您分配主管，他们可以检查和批准时间表、分配任务、跟踪休假时间、安排项目等等，所有这些都可以在应用程序内完成。 如果需要附加组件，请确保它们便宜且易于集成。

定价：检查付费层中可用的功能，这样您就不需要在适当的时候移动应用程序来支付合适的时间跟踪应用程序。

可用的集成：确保时间跟踪应用程序集成到您已经使用的工作流程中，并查看它可以与哪些平台集成，尤其是在您选择免费计划的情况下。

客户支持：在选择时间跟踪应用程序之前，请检查提供客户支持的不同方式。 他们有培训视频吗？ 是否有可以解决问题的论坛？ 如果问题变得特别具有挑战性，客户支持主管是否可以介入？

时间跟踪应用程序的好处

各种规模的企业的免费时间跟踪应用程序的一些好处包括：

  • 跟踪员工的工作小时数
  • 准确计费客户
  • 清楚地传达项目进度
  • 保持透明度
  • 识别时间槽并提高生产力
  • 主动识别项目延迟

您的企业需要时间跟踪应用程序吗？

无论企业规模如何，如果需要关于产品/项目定价、计划日期或安排整个团队的明智和基于数据的决策，时间跟踪应用程序可能会很有用。

一旦被暂停，jcard 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，jcards 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jcards 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jcards 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 jcards 的方法：

降低 jcards 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jcards 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

   

If you are unfamiliar with employee time tracking or have been manually tracking time until now, utilising free time tracking apps can be a cost-effective way to learn about these apps' functionalities. However, with a plethora of time tracking apps available in the market, each with varying features and pricing, selecting the appropriate solution can be daunting. To assist with this decision, we have identified the seven most suitable time tracking apps for small businesses.

To aid you in your search for the ideal time tracking app, we have curated a collection of the top free time tracking apps of 2023.

JCards : recognized for its overall best performance
TimeCamp: considered the best for remote teams
Toggl Track: ideal for very small teams
Paymo: preferred for invoicing
My Hours: best suited for freelancers
Deputy: noted for its exceptional scheduling capabilities
TrackingTime: favoured for its visual time tracking features

JCards -

Price Range: Free For 30 Days(no Credit Card Required) $10 per user per month

User Limit: Unlimited

Supported Platforms:
Web app for Edge, Chrome and Firefox; Desktop apps for Linux, Mac and Windows; Mobile app for iOS and Android.

Why We Chose It:

JCards was chosen as the top overall time tracking app due to its comprehensive cloud-based time tracking features. With JCards, users can easily track time on various projects, clients, and devices. The app also allows for attendance tracking, payroll and accounting optimization, billable time and expense tracking, and the ability to share project progress with clients.

JCards is also one of the most cost-effective paid time tracking solutions available, with its paid tiers starting at $10 per user per month,with no contract. These plans offer additional features such as the ability to add time for others, perform time audits, include breaks for employees, and more.

Who should use it:

JCards is a great fit for field trade businesses, such as construction companies, painters, electricians, and others.

Pros:

  • JCards include both manual and automatic tracking,
  • an intuitive timesheet,
  • reminders for overdue tasks,
  • offline time clocking,
  • an integrated calendar,
  • project tracking,
  • in-depth reporting and filtering,
  • GPS location tracking,
  • invoicing,
  • record-keeping for expenses,
  • timesheet approval,
  • time off tracking.

Cons:

While JCards offers a comprehensive set of features, some users may find that the app lacks advanced customization options. Additionally, as JCards primarily focuses on time tracking and project management, it may not be the best fit for businesses with more complex needs, such as those requiring extensive customer relationship management or accounting functionality.

TimeCamp:

Pros: Excellent for remote teams with advanced monitoring and productivity features Automated time tracking and timesheet approvals

Customizable billing rates and invoicing

Cons: The user interface can be overwhelming for some users Certain features may require additional integration with other apps or services

The mobile app may lack some functionality compared to the web app

Toggl Track:

Pros:

Very easy to use with a simple and intuitive interface Ideal for very small teams or freelancers with limited needs

Integrates with a wide range of apps and services

Cons:

May lack some advanced features required by larger businesses or teams Invoicing functionality is limited in the free version

Some users may prefer a more detailed and robust reporting system

Paymo:

Pros:

Excellent invoicing and project management features Includes a wide range of tools for team collaboration and communication

Customizable dashboard with various project metrics

Cons:

More expensive than other options on this list, especially for larger teams Some users may find the interface cluttered or difficult to navigate

Certain features may be lacking in the lower pricing tiers

My Hours:

Pros:

Ideal for freelancers with simple time tracking needs Customizable project and task tracking

Automated invoicing and reporting features

Cons:

May lack the advanced features required by larger teams or businesses Some users may find the interface simplistic or lacking in visual appeal

Integration with other apps and services may be limited

Deputy:

Pros:

Exceptional scheduling and employee management features Customizable timesheets and automated payroll calculations

Includes a range of tools for team communication and task management

Cons:

More expensive than some other options on this list, especially for larger teams Certain features may require additional setup or configuration

Some users may find the interface complex or difficult to navigate

TrackingTime:

Pros:

Favored for its visual time tracking features Includes a range of tools for team collaboration and project management

Customizable reporting and invoicing options

Cons:

May lack some advanced features required by larger teams or businesses Some users may find the interface overly simplistic or lacking in certain functionality

Certain features may be limited in the free version

How To Choose a Free Time Tracking App

Choosing the right time tracking app can be frustrating, given the numerous similar apps available in the market. Here are a few pointers to guide you in your search. Evaluate all the apps you are considering on the following parameters to choose the best one for your team.

Ease of Use: Evaluate how easy it is to use the app to ensure widespread adoption and success of the app in your organisation.

Time Tracking and Reporting: Ensure that the app can track different parameters, including clock-in/clock-out timing, time of, billable and non-billable hours, and provide robust reporting on these parameters, which can then be used to assess employee productivity and impact.

Team Management Capabilities: Check if the app allows you to assign supervisors who can check and approve timesheets, assign tasks, track time off, schedule projects, and more, all from within the app. If add-ons are required, ensure that they are cheap and easy to integrate.

Pricing: Check the features available in paid tiers so that you do not need to move apps when the time is right to pay for a suitable time tracking app.

Available Integrations: Ensure that the time tracking app integrates into the workflows you are already using and see what platforms it can integrate with, especially since you are choosing a free plan.

Customer Support: Before choosing a time tracking app, check the different ways in which customer support is available. Do they have training videos? Are there forums where you can get your problems solved? Is a customer support executive available for stepping in if the problem gets especially challenging?

Benefits of Time Tracking App

Some benefits of free time tracking apps for businesses of all sizes include:

  • Tracking the number of hours worked by employees
  • Billing clients accurately
  • Communicating project progress clearly
  • Maintaining transparency
  • Identifying time sinks and improving productivity
  • Proactively identifying project delays

Does your business need a time tracking app?

Regardless of the business size, if informed and data-based decisions about pricing products/projects, planning days or scheduling the entire team are needed, a time tracking app can be useful.

Once suspended, jcards will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jcards will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jcards will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jcards is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag jcards:

Make all posts by jcards less visible

jcards consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging jcards will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: jcards

item.title: 在 WiFi 网络中共享打印机 | Sharing a Printer in a WiFi Network
item.description:
打印机可能不是我们应该在家里拥有的东西，至少当我们可以在工作、联合办公空间或复印店使用共享打印机时。

没有开发人员喜欢修理打印机！ 众所周知，打印机昂贵、浪费，而且肯定会因缺少、不兼容或过时的设备驱动程序而造成麻烦。 您不想成为必须支持和解决其他人的打印问题的“IT 专家”，这已成为一个流行的笑话。

我在家里使用 HP LaserJet 时遇到了问题。 至少，打印机不拒绝使用第三方彩色墨盒，所以我们可以使用回收的墨盒来代替更昂贵的原装墨盒。

过时的驱动程序，计划淘汰？ 但是我经常挣扎并且无法为 Windows 以外的任何其他平台安装驱动程序。 几年前它曾经工作过，也许是 2018 年他们最后一次更新HPLIP驱动程序时？

甚至不再在 Linux 上工作了？！

但是在 Apple 和 Linux 都将其基于CUPS的打印系统切换到 Python 3 或其他一些我不介意理解的重大变化之后，打印文档的唯一方法似乎是使用运行 Microsoft Windows 的 PC。 所以我不得不将 PDF 保存在云端或通过电子邮件发送给自己，然后在物理连接到打印机的笔记本电脑上启动 Windows，开始打印过程，检查纸张是否已成功打印，然后关闭 Windows。 多么浪费时间和精力！

后来，我遇到了另一个硬件问题，当时德国 Telefonica 子公司 O² 向我们发送了他们的默认白标路由器，当我的 Linux 笔记本电脑访问该路由器时，该路由器似乎使用了缓慢的回退协议。 所以我需要一个替代方案。

重复使用二手硬件🪄✨🙏

后来我发现路由器有一个选项可以通过 USB 连接打印机并使其作为网络打印机访问。

无需再进行故障排除！ 现在我们的旧 LaserJet 已连接到路由器，每个登录到我们的 WiFi 网络的人都可以将其用作网络打印机。

一旦暂停，ingosteinke 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，ingosteinke 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ingosteinke 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ingosteinke 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 ingosteinke：

使 ingosteinke 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 ingosteinke 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Printers are maybe not something we should own at our home, at least not when we can access a shared printer at work, in a coworking space or a copyshop.

No Developer likes to Fix Printers! 🔧🖨️😒

Printers are notorious to be expensive, wasteful, and guaranteed to cause trouble due to missing, incompatible, or outdated device drivers. It has become a running gag that you don't want to be the "IT expert" having to support and fix other people's printing problems.

I have had trouble using an HP LaserJet in our family. At least, the printer doesn't refuse to work with third-party color cartridges, so we can use recycled ones instead of the more expensive original ones.

Outdated Drivers, Planned Obsolescence? 🗑️💸

But I often struggled and failed to install a driver for any other platform than Windows. It used to work some years ago, maybe 2018 when they last updated their HPLIP driver?

Not even working on Linux anymore?!

But after both Apple and Linux switched their CUPS-based printing systems to Python 3 or some other breaking change that I don't mind to understand, the only way to print a document seemed to be using a PC running Microsoft Windows. So I had to save a PDF in the cloud or email it to myself, then startup Windows on a laptop physically connected to the printer, start the printing process, check if the paper has been printed successfully, and shut down Windows. What a waste of time and energy!

Later, I had another hardware problem, when the German Telefonica subsidiary O² had sent us their default white label router which seemed to use a slow fall back protocol when accessed by my Linux laptop. So I needed an alternative.

Reusing Second-Hand Hardware 🪄✨🙏

The Fritz!Box 7520 is branded by 1&1 and cheap to get second-hand, and it works as an O² client as well, as a helpful forum user pointed out.

Later I found out that the router has an option to connect a printer via USB and make it accessible as a network printer.

No More Troubleshooting! ✌️

Now our old LaserJet is connected to the router and can be used as a network printer by everyone logged into our WiFi network.

Once suspended, ingosteinke will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ingosteinke will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ingosteinke will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ingosteinke is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ingosteinke:

Make all posts by ingosteinke less visible

ingosteinke consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ingosteinke will restore default visibility to their posts.
