item.title: 选择 MQTT Broker 2023 时要考虑的 7 个因素 | 7 Factors to Consider When Choosing MQTT Broker 2023
item.description:
因此，选择合适的 MQTT Broker 是构建物联网应用程序的第一步，也是最关键的一步。 本文根据典型的 IoT 项目要求和场景概述了一般选择注意事项和潜在问题。 通过深入研究这些因素，读者将了解如何选择符合他们独特需求和要求的 MQTT 代理。

确定项目的要求

市场上提供了多种 MQTT 代理，从私有部署选项到支持 MQTT 连接的基于云的服务。

有很多选择会给你更多的选择，但也会因为增加的复杂性而变得更难做出决定。

没有做出最佳决策的通用公式。 但是，以下问题可能会指导您进行选择：

  1. 从长远来看，设备的预期规模是多少？
  2. 什么是必要的性能指标？ 消息延迟和可靠性对您的项目有多重要？
  3. 您将如何利用这些数据？
  4. 您的用户和物联网设备位于何处？
  5. 你的数据有什么特点？ 消息大小和频率是否是必要的考虑因素？
  6. 您如何在您的应用程序中处理物联网数据，包括首选编程语言以及数据存储和分析组件？
  7. 相关行业有没有广泛使用的MQTT Broker？
  8. 您有付费服务的预算吗？
  9. 这些问题将帮助您确定您需要从 MQTT Broker 获得的特性和功能。 以下部分将更详细地讨论 MQTT Broker 的特性，以帮助您为您的项目选择最合适的 MQTT Broker。

    首先，让我们了解一下 MQTT Broker 是如何运作的。

    消息被传递给所有订阅了该主题的订阅者。

    下图显示了发布-订阅模型如何在多个订阅者（可以是设备或应用程序）之间实现消息分发。

    学习MQTT 发布-订阅模式。

    这些是客户端和 MQTT Broker 在消息传递时采取的步骤：

    1. 建立连接：发布者客户端和订阅者客户端都发起连接请求，与MQTT Broker建立连接。
    2. 订阅主题：订阅者客户端订阅一个或多个主题。
    3. 消息发布：发布者客户端指定发布消息的主题和负载。
    4. 消息路由：当 Broker 收到一条消息时，它会检查订阅者列表并将消息路由到所有已订阅该主题的客户端。
    5. 断开连接：客户端发起断开连接的请求。 大多数 MQTT 代理实现 MQTT 协议中指定的基本功能以支持基本消息传递功能，例如 QoS 级别、客户端身份验证、保留消息、共享订阅等。 这些功能有助于快速实施特定用例。

      然而，不仅如此。 如果把 MQTT Broker 比作一个港口，那么消息传递只是货物的运输。 事实上，要保证货物的运输，需要完善的物流体系和仓储设施来提供必不可少的支持。 将货物从各地发往不同的目的地，需要对货物进行拆包和重新包装，并采用不同的物流方式进行派送。 在物流淡季和旺季，需要动态灵活地调整港口设施和人员规模，以满足需求的同时实现效率最大化。

      在 MQTT Broker 的上下文中，这些需求对应于基本操作的安全性、故障处理和指标监控，MQTT 消息传递的数据处理和集成能力，以及整个服务的可扩展性。 这些功能对于构建满足不同需求的企业级物联网应用程序至关重要。

      安全

      安全性是选择 MQTT Broker 时要考虑的关键因素，需要牢记以下几个方面。

      客户端认证

      以下是 MQTT Broker 常用的认证方式及其要求：

      身份验证方法描述功能需求
      用户名密码当客户端连接时，它会提供特定的用户名和密码。 服务器收到后会进行验证并决定是否允许连接。 与多个数据库和现有数据兼容。 支持自定义身份验证和与现有企业身份验证服务的集成。
      服务器不存储客户端身份验证或会话信息。 客户端提供用于身份验证的签名令牌。 支持JWKs和一套完整的加密算法。
      客户端和服务器使用 TLS/SSL 协议进行安全通信，避免窃听和数据丢失，同时使用 X.509 客户端证书进行身份验证。 使用 SSL/TLS 协议提供安全通信。 通过 OCSP 装订/CRL 验证提供低成本的证书检查。

      发布订阅授权

      授权是在客户端可以发布或订阅之前验证客户端对特定主题的操作权限的过程。 权限列表通常存储在内部或外部的数据库中，需要实时更新以反映业务变化。

      以下是授权功能的一些典型需求：

      功能需求描述
      细粒度的访问控制能够满足不同层次的门禁需求。
      支持缓存由于发布和订阅是高频操作，缓存机制可以有效缓解高峰期的压力。
      支持与多个数据库集成用户可以灵活选择自己熟悉的技术栈。
      与当前数据库中存在的数据兼容从旧系统迁移时请考虑这一点。

      软件漏洞和企业 IT 安全

      软件行业从过去的经验中获得了宝贵的见解，认识到软件中的安全漏洞会严重影响企业的运营。

      如果您计划在生产环境中使用 MQTT Broker，使用广泛接受的安全验证方法进行严格的安全评估以确保其符合安全标准至关重要：

      • 开源验证：检查Broker的代码开放程度和开源社区的验证水平；
      • 安全集成：检查安全测试和防护是否充分，是否采用了专业的安全解决方案。

      企业 IT 安全对于保护企业数据免受各种安全威胁（例如数据泄露、破坏、盗窃和滥用）至关重要。 为确保最佳安全性，MQTT 代理必须提供密码策略、安全审计、数据加密和其他相关安全功能。

      阅读我们的博客系列2023 年 MQTT 安全性 7 大须知，了解更多关于 MQTT 安全性的信息。

      集群和自动缩放

      客户端可以作为一个统一的实体与集群交互，而无需了解内部工作原理或节点数量的任何变化。 集群处理连接，也像单个节点一样发布和订阅消息。

      为什么我们需要 MQTT Broker 集群？

      确保更大的连接和更高的可扩展性

      想象一下，您有一辆配备了物联网功能的汽车。 随着它涌入市场，每月销量达到数千到数万台，您的 MQTT 代理需要为未来几年可能出现的连接激增做好准备，从数万到数百万不等。 随着车载系统的 OTA 升级，更多的数据将被传输到云端，从而导致 MQTT Broker 的消息吞吐量显着增加。

      借助支持集群的 MQTT Broker，您可以在运行时向集群添加节点，从而轻松进行水平扩展以适应数量不断增加的 MQTT 消息和客户端连接。

      保证服务的高可用

      并非每个应用程序都需要处理业务扩展的需求。 例如，如果您的业务仅专注于特定学校或制造设施的环境监测，那么即使没有任何改变，客户和消息的数量也有望在数年内保持一致。

      您可能已经注意到，一个单独的 MQTT 代理可以容纳数万个客户端，这足以满足大多数物联网应用程序的需求。 鉴于此，是否有必要实施集群？

      它的确是。 因此，如果您需要您的应用程序可靠，您应该选择支持集群的 MQTT Broker。

      只有少数 MQTT 代理支持集群

      它还旨在平衡连接的负载，集中管理设备，并确保整个集群的可扩展性和高可用性。

      实现所有这些功能是一项复杂的工作，这就是为什么大多数 MQTT 代理的部署仅限于单个节点。 然而，认识到可扩展性和高可用性的重要性，其中一些代理提供了专门的实施解决方案。

      使用 MQTT 桥接功能链接多个代理

      使用 MQTT 消息发布和订阅在多个 Brokers 之间交换消息提供了一定程度的可扩展性，允许更多客户端相互连接和交互。 然而，众所周知，这种通信模式效率极低，并且不能确保高可用性。

      在多个节点之间同步完整会话状态

      如果一个节点出现故障，负载均衡可以快速切换到另一个节点，提供单机热备份，避免对物联网应用造成任何中断。 但是，请务必注意，此方法可能不是扩展的最佳选择，并且可能导致成本增加。

      尽管上述解决方案是可行的，但它并不能同时提供可扩展性和高可用性，而且还会使部署过程复杂化。 为了简化可根据需要扩展的可靠物联网服务的创建，建议选择支持自身集群的 MQTT 代理。

      了解 EMQX 如何使用高效的集群实现单个集群的 100M 连接。

      使用 EMQX 5.0 达到 100M MQTT 连接

      数据集成和规则引擎

      在开发 IoT 应用程序时，通常必须在多个系统之间实现数据交换，而不仅仅是在设备之间。

      例如，从工厂生产线上的传感器收集的数据可以通过 MQTT Broker 发送到配套的 MES 和 ERP 系统。 要在两个系统之间建立可靠的连接，数据库或事件驱动的消息队列（如 Apache Kafka）可以充当桥梁。

      同样，可以收集分散在城市各处的天气传感器数据并将其存储在时间序列数据库（如 InfluxDB）中进行全面分析，从而充分发挥数据的潜力。

      一种简单的方法是创建一个应用程序，该应用程序订阅来自MQTT 主题的消息并将它们发送到相关的数据集成。 一些 MQTT 代理以插件或扩展的形式提供此功能，因为这是一个常见的要求。

      您可能还需要在保存数据之前对数据进行过滤、编码或其他处理，以满足实际业务需求。

      一些 MQTT 代理提供了一个内置的规则引擎，通过使用户能够在代理上创建数据驱动的规则并将结果发送到数据集成来促进数据处理。 通常，可以使用 SQL 或表单等低代码工具配置此功能。

      整个过程如下图所示：

      对于需要与外部数据系统集成的物联网应用程序，拥有内置的数据集成和规则引擎可能是 MQTT 代理的主要优势。 此功能减少了对额外开发工作的需求，可以加快业务交付，同时还可以在集群内实现自动扩展以实现高可用性。

      表现

      最大连接数：MQTT Broker 可以支持的最大客户端连接数。

    6. 消息传输延迟：消息从发送方发送到接收方所花费的时间，主要取决于 MQTT Broker 的性能，假设网络环境一致。
    7. 消息发送/接收速率：MQTT Broker 每秒可以发送或接收的消息数。
    8. 消息存储性能：此指标与支持消息持久性和外部数据集成的 MQTT 代理相关，因为它衡量消息存储功能的性能。

    虽然性能很重要，但它并不是在 MQTT 代理中寻找的唯一因素。 表现出色的经纪人通常在其他领域也表现出色，但不要仅仅依靠绩效指标来对其进行评估，除非表现明显不佳。

    此外，需要注意的是，MQTT Broker 报告的性能指标是基于特定场景的，消息速率、主题级别、消息 QoS、消息负载大小和规则引擎状态等各种因素都会影响结果。

    此外，任何 Broker 都可以声明难以复制且与用户无关的性能指标。 如果您有高性能需求，请仔细检查其技术是否可以达到预期的结果，以及其测试结果是否可以重现。 真知灼见源于实践，所以最好结合自己的应用场景进行压力测试。

    云原生

    通过利用 Cloud-Native 技术，MQTT Broker 和基础设施可以无缝集成，通过使用容器、微服务和自动化运维来实现高效、灵活和可靠的部署。

    此外，云原生技术还提供配置管理、集群伸缩、无缝升级、故障处理、统一监控等管理能力，助力大规模物联网应用的开发和运营。

    为了实现这些目标，将 MQTT Broker 的可扩展性和管理功能与云基础架构的底层功能紧密集成至关重要。 然而，在现实中，不同 Broker 的 Cloud-Native 实现水平各不相同。

    使用EMQX Kubernetes Operator自动创建、配置和管理 MQTT 集群。

    支持扩展

    单一的软件解决方案无法满足所有用户的需求。 为了满足特定需求，例如支持多种消息传输协议、自定义身份验证和授权、专用数据加密方法以及监控和警报功能，可能需要扩展 MQTT Broker 的功能。

    为了促进这一点，MQTT Broker 必须提供适当的扩展机制，例如插件架构，以便在需要时启用自定义。 此外，它应该支持用于扩展开发的主流编程语言。

    学习MQTT 在各种客户端中的最佳实践。

    成本

    成本是一个多方面的考虑因素，必须根据您的预算进行评估。

    根据您的需要，您可以选择企业服务或开源 MQTT Broker。 有许多可用的开源 MQTT 代理，如果开源许可证允许，通常可以部署它们而无需支付任何许可费用。 但是，安装、维护和扩展开发可能需要额外的资源。

    为大型应用程序部署 MQTT 代理时，必须评估其性能并考虑与之相关的成本。 高性能 MQTT 代理可以显着减少与硬件、网络和维护相关的开销，从而降低总体成本。

    选择托管MQTT 云服务时，必须了解计费方式，因为它通常取决于连接数和流量。 您应该仔细查看每个计费选项的详细信息，并为您的特定用例选择最具成本效益的选项。

    其他注意事项

    除了 MQTT Broker 本身，还有其他几个因素需要考虑：

    • 提供增强型本地化服务

    选择提供区域或全球服务的 MQTT Broker 提供商，因为他们可以帮助企业获得更快的技术支持、加速交付并大幅降低成本。

    • 避免从头开始构建系统

    在为 IoT 项目选择 MQTT 代理或技术时，为了最大限度地降低投资风险和成本，建议选择在行业中具有良好记录的成熟解决方案。 应避免从头开始开发系统，因为这可能耗时、成本高且存在潜在风险。

    • 支持边云无缝融合

    在边缘部署时，选择资源消耗低、针对边缘环境进行了优化或具有经过验证的云边缘解决方案的 MQTT Broker 非常重要。

    在本文中，我们概述了开发人员在为其项目选择 MQTT 代理时应考虑的关键标准。 通过根据他们的具体要求仔细评估每个标准，读者可以做出明智的决定并选择最适合他们需求的 MQTT Broker。

    最初发表于www.emqx.com 。



原文:

The MQTT Broker plays a crucial role in facilitating messaging between IoT devices, making it a key component in IoT applications. As such, selecting the appropriate MQTT Broker serves as the initial and most critical step in building IoT applications. This article outlines general selection considerations and potential concerns based on typical IoT project requirements and scenarios. By delving into these factors, readers will understand how to choose an MQTT Broker that aligns with their unique needs and requirements.

Identify the Requirements of Your Project

The market offers a variety of MQTT brokers, from private deployment options to cloud-based services that enable MQTT connectivity.

Having a lot of choices gives you more options, but it also makes it more difficult to decide because of the increased complexity.

There is no universal formula for making the best decision. However, the following questions may guide you in the selection process:

  1. What is the expected scale of devices in the long run?
  2. What are the necessary performance metrics? How essential are message latency and reliability for your project?
  3. Where will the MQTT Broker be located? How will you leverage the data?
  4. Where your users and IoT devices are geographically located?
  5. What are the characteristics of your data? Is message size and frequency a necessary consideration?
  6. How do you process IoT data in your application, including the preferred programming language and the data storage and analysis components?
  7. Is there a widely used MQTT Broker in the relevant industry?
  8. Do you have a budget for paid services?
  9. …

These questions will help you identify the features and functionalities that you need from the MQTT Broker. The following section will discuss MQTT Broker features in more detail to help you choose the most suitable MQTT Broker for your project.

How the MQTT Broker Works

First, let's comprehend how the MQTT Broker operates.

The MQTT Broker uses the publish-subscribe messaging model, which means that a message publisher client sends a message to a specific topic, and a message subscriber client receives the message by subscribing to the same topic. The message is delivered to all subscribers who have subscribed to the topic.

The diagram below shows how the publish-subscribe model enables message distribution among multiple subscribers, which can be devices or applications.

Learn MQTT Publish-subscribe Pattern.

These are the steps that the client and MQTT Broker take when messaging:

  1. Establish a connection: Both the publisher and subscriber clients initiate a connection request to establish a connection with the MQTT Broker.
  2. Subscribe to topics: The subscriber client subscribes to one or more topics.
  3. Message publishing: The publisher client specifies the topic and payload to publish the message.
  4. Message routing: When the Broker receives a message, it checks the list of subscribers and routes the message to all clients that have subscribed to the topic.
  5. Disconnect: The client initiates a request to disconnect. The MQTT Broker can also disconnect from the client after a network exception or heartbeat expiration.

Most MQTT Brokers implement the fundamental features specified in the MQTT protocol to support basic messaging functionality, such as QoS levels, client authentication, retained messages, shared subscriptions, and more. These features facilitate the rapid implementation of specific use cases.

However, there is more to it than that. If the MQTT Broker is like a port, message delivery is only the transportation of goods. In fact, to ensure the transportation of goods, a complete logistics system and storage facilities are required to provide essential support. To send goods from various places to different destinations, it is necessary to unpack and repack the goods and use different logistics methods to deliver them. In the off-season and peak season of logistics, it is necessary to adjust the scale of port facilities and personnel dynamically and flexibly to meet demand while maximizing efficiency.

In the context of MQTT Broker, these requirements correspond to security, fault handling, and metrics monitoring for basic operation, data processing and integration capabilities for MQTT messaging, and scalability for the entire service. These features are essential for building enterprise-class IoT applications that cater to diverse needs.

Security

Security is a key factor to consider when choosing an MQTT Broker, and the following aspects are important to keep in mind.

Client Authentication

The MQTT client authentication requires clients to provide specific credentials to confirm their identity when connecting to the MQTT Broker. Here are the commonly used authentication methods and their requirements for the MQTT Broker:

Authentication Method Description Functional Requirement
Username/Password When the client connects, it provides a specific username and password. After receiving it, the server will verify and decide whether to allow the connection. Compatible with multiple databases and existing data. Support custom authentication and integration with existing enterprise authentication services.
JWT Authentication The server does not store client authentication or session information. The client provides a signed token for authentication. Support JWKs and a complete set of encryption algorithms.
X.509 Certificate The client and server use the TLS/SSL protocol for secure communication to avoid eavesdropping and data loss, while using X.509 client certificates for authentication. Provide secure communication with SSL/TLS protocols. Offer low-cost certificate checking with OCSP Stapling/CRL validation.

Authorization for Publishing and Subscribing

Authorization is the process of verifying the operational privileges of clients for a specific topic before they can publish or subscribe. Permission lists are usually stored in databases, either internal or external, and need to be updated in real time to reflect business changes.

The following are some typical requirements for the authorization function:

Functional Requirement Description
Fine-grained access control Able to meet the access control needs of various levels.
Support caching As publishing and subscribing are high-frequency operations, a caching mechanism can effectively alleviate pressure during peak hours.
Support integration with multiple databases Users can flexibly choose their own familiar technology stack.
Compatible with the data present in the current database Consider this when migrating from an old system.

Software Vulnerabilities and Enterprise IT Security

The software industry has gained valuable insights from past experiences, recognizing that security vulnerabilities in software can substantially affect an enterprise's operations.

If you plan to use an MQTT Broker in a production environment, it is vital to conduct a rigorous security evaluation using the widely accepted security validation methods to ensure its compliance with security standards:

  • Open source validation: check the Broker's code openness and the level of its validation by the open source community;
  • Security integration: check the sufficiency of security testing and protection, and the adoption of professional security solutions.

Enterprise IT security is essential to safeguard enterprise data from various security threats such as data leakage, destruction, theft, and abuse. To ensure optimal security, password policies, security audits, data encryption, and other relevant security features must be offered by MQTT Brokers.

Discover more about MQTT security by reading our blog series 7 Essential Things to Know about MQTT Security 2023.

Clustering and Auto-Scaling

An MQTT Broker cluster is a system that distributes the workload of connecting and messaging among multiple MQTT Brokers (also known as nodes).

Clients can interact with the cluster as a unified entity, without being aware of the internal workings or any changes in the number of nodes. The cluster handles connections and also publishes and subscribes messages just like a single node.

Why Do We Need MQTT Broker Cluster?

Ensure Larger Connections and Higher Scalability

Imagine that you have a car equipped with IoT capabilities. As it floods the market, with monthly sales reaching thousands to tens of thousands of units, your MQTT Broker needs to prepare for a potential surge in connections, ranging from tens of thousands to millions, in the coming years. With the OTA upgrades of the vehicle system, more data is expected to be transmitted to the cloud, causing a significant increase in message throughput for your MQTT Broker.

With a cluster-supported MQTT Broker, you have the ability to add nodes to the cluster at runtime, enabling easy horizontal scaling to accommodate an increasing number of MQTT messages and client connections.

Guarantee high availability of services

Not every application is required to handle the demands of business expansion. For instance, if your business is solely focused on environmental monitoring in a particular school or manufacturing facility, the volume of clients and messages can be anticipated to remain consistent for several years, even without any alterations.

You might have noticed that a solitary MQTT broker has the capacity to accommodate tens of thousands of clients, which is adequate for the majority of IoT applications. In light of this, is it essential to implement clustering?

Indeed, it is. An MQTT Broker cluster can persist in functioning even when certain node failures, guaranteeing that there is no single point of failure and that the service remains accessible at all times.

Therefore, if you need your application to be reliable, you should select an MQTT Broker that supports clustering.

Only a Few MQTT Brokers Support Clustering

An MQTT Broker cluster's main responsibility is to synchronize and replicate MQTT session state, such as subscribed messages and pending message transfers, among cluster nodes in an efficient and reliable way. It also aims to balance the load of connections, manage devices centrally, and ensure the scalability and high availability of the whole cluster.

Implementing all these features is a complex undertaking, which is why the deployment of most MQTT Brokers is restricted to a single node. However, recognizing the significance of scalability and high availability, some of these Brokers offer specialized implementation solutions.

Use MQTT Bridging Feature to Link Multiple Brokers

Using MQTT message publishing and subscribing to exchange messages among multiple Brokers provides some level of scalability, allowing more clients to connect and interact with each other. However, this mode of communication is known to be highly inefficient and does not ensure high availability.

Synchronize Full Session State Between Multiple Nodes

One way to achieve high availability of MQTT Brokers is to run multiple instances simultaneously and perform full synchronization of session state among nodes. If a node fails, load balancing can quickly switch to another node, providing single-machine hot backups and avoiding any disruption to IoT applications. However, it is important to note that this method may not be the best choice for scaling and can lead to increased costs.

Although the above solutions are functional, it does not provide both scalability and high availability at the same time, and can also complicate the deployment process. To simplify the creation of reliable IoT services that can scale as needed, it is recommended to choose MQTT Brokers that support clustering themselves.

Find out how EMQX achieved 100M connections with a single cluster using efficient clustering.

Reaching 100M MQTT connections with EMQX 5.0

Data Integration and Rule Engine

When developing IoT applications, it's often essential to enable data exchange among multiple systems, not only between devices.

For instance, data collected from sensors on a factory line can be sent to accompanying MES and ERP systems via an MQTT Broker. To establish a reliable connection between the two systems, a database or an event-driven message queue like Apache Kafka can serve as a bridge.

Similarly, weather sensor data scattered across a city can be gathered and stored in a time-series database like InfluxDB for thorough analysis, enabling the data's full potential to be realized.

A simple way to do this is to create an application that subscribes to messages from an MQTT topic and sends them to the relevant data integration. Some MQTT Brokers offer this functionality as plugins or extensions, since this is a common requirement.

You may also need to filter, encode, or otherwise process the data before saving it to meet the actual business needs.

Some MQTT Brokers offer a built-in rule engine that facilitates data processing by enabling users to create data-driven rules on the Broker and send results to data integration. Typically, this functionality can be configured using low-code tools such as SQL or form.

The entire process is shown in the following diagram:

For IoT applications that require integration with external data systems, having a built-in data integration and rule engine can be a major advantage of MQTT Brokers. This feature reduces the need for additional development work and can speed up business delivery, while also enabling auto-scaling within the cluster for high availability.

Performance

MQTT Broker is used to connect a large number of clients and enable massive messaging, where the following performance metrics need to be considered:

  1. Maximum number of connections: which refers to the maximum number of client connections that the MQTT Broker can support.
  2. Message transmission latency: the time it takes for a message to be sent from the sender to the receiver, and is primarily dependent on the performance of the MQTT Broker, assuming a consistent network environment.
  3. Message send/receive rate: the number of messages that can be sent or received per second by the MQTT Broker.
  4. Message storage performance: this metric is relevant for MQTT Brokers that support message persistence with external data integration, as it measures the performance of the message storage functionality.

While performance is essential, it's not the only thing to look for in an MQTT Broker. A high-performing broker usually excels in other areas too, but don't rely solely on performance metrics to evaluate it, unless it's significantly underperforming.

Besides, it is important to note that the performance metrics reported by an MQTT Broker are based on a specific scenario, and various factors such as message rate, topic level, message QoS, message payload size, and the status of the rule engine can influence the outcomes.

Moreover, any Broker can claim a performance metric that is hard to replicate and irrelevant to the users. If you have high performance demands, please carefully examine whether its technology can deliver the expected results and whether its test results can be reproduced. Real knowledge comes from practice, so it is best to conduct a stress test based on your own application scenarios.

Cloud Native

Cloud-Native is a modern software architecture and delivery approach specifically designed to facilitate the efficient and reliable creation and deployment of cloud-based applications.

By leveraging Cloud-Native technology, MQTT Broker and infrastructure can be seamlessly integrated to enable efficient, flexible, and reliable deployment through the use of containers, microservices, and automated operations and maintenance.

Additionally, Cloud-Native technology offers management capabilities such as configuration management, cluster scaling, seamless upgrades, fault handling, and unified monitoring to enhance the development and operation of large-scale IoT applications.

To achieve these goals, it is crucial for the scalability and management functionalities of the MQTT Broker to be tightly integrated with the underlying capabilities of the cloud infrastructure. However, in reality, the level of Cloud-Native implementation varies among different Brokers.

Automate the creation, configuration, and management of your MQTT cluster with EMQX Kubernetes Operator.

Support Extensions

A single software solution cannot fulfill the requirements of all users. To accommodate specific requirements, such as support for multiple message transfer protocols, custom authentication and authorization, specialized data encryption methods, and monitoring and alert capabilities, it may be necessary to extend the functionality of the MQTT Broker.

To facilitate this, the MQTT Broker must provide an appropriate extension mechanism, such as a plugin architecture, to enable customization when needed. Additionally, it should support mainstream programming languages for extension development.

Learn best practice of MQTT in various clients.

Cost

Cost is a multifaceted consideration that must be evaluated in relation to your budget.

Depending on your needs, you may opt for enterprise services or an open-source MQTT Broker. There are many open-source MQTT Brokers available, which can typically be deployed without incurring any licensing fees if the open-source license allows it. However, installation, maintenance, and extension development may require additional resources.

When deploying MQTT Brokers for large-scale applications, it's essential to assess their performance and consider the costs associated with them. High-performance MQTT Brokers can significantly reduce the overheads associated with hardware, network, and maintenance, resulting in lower overall costs.

When choosing a managed MQTT cloud service, it is essential to understand how billing works, as it usually depends on the number of connections and the amount of traffic. You should review the details of each billing option carefully and select the most cost-effective option for your specific use case.

Additional Considerations

In addition to the MQTT Broker itself, there are several other factors to consider:

  • Provide Enhanced and Localized Services

Choose MQTT Broker providers who deliver regional or global services, as they help enterprises to get quicker technical support, accelerate delivery and cut costs substantially.

  • Avoid Building Systems From Scratch

To minimize investment risks and costs when selecting MQTT Brokers or technologies for IoT projects, it is advisable to opt for established solutions with a proven track record in the industry. Developing systems from scratch should be avoided as it can be time-consuming, costly, and potentially risky.

  • Support Seamless Integration of Edge and Cloud

When deploying at the edge, it is important to select an MQTT Broker that has low resource consumption, is optimized for edge environments, or has a proven cloud-edge solution.

In this article, we have outlined the key criteria that developers should consider when selecting an MQTT Broker for their projects. By carefully evaluating each criterion based on their specific requirements, readers can make an informed decision and choose the MQTT Broker that best fits their needs.

Originally published at www.emqx.com.
item.author: emqtech

item.title: 你好呀 | Hi there
item.description:
一旦暂停，修复者将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，修复者将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，修复者的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 fixerrs 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 fixerrs 的操作：

使 fixerrs 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 fixerrs 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, fixerrs will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, fixerrs will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by fixerrs will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If fixerrs is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag fixerrs:

Make all posts by fixerrs less visible

fixerrs consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging fixerrs will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 如何使用 Bunzz 的用户触发部署 SDK 进行 DApp 开发 | How to Use the Bunzz’s User-Triggered Deployment SDK for DApp Development
item.description:
去中心化应用程序 (DApps) 有可能改变我们与技术交互和进行交易的方式。 然而，在区块链上构建和部署智能合约对开发人员来说可能既耗时又具有挑战性。

本文将讨论合约克隆问题、用户 Triggered Deployment SDK 的好处，以及如何使用它。

合同克隆的问题

有时，DApp 需要用户多次部署或克隆同一个合约。 例如，DAO 平台等无代码 web3 工具、Uniswap 等协议和 NFT 市场允许用户创建自己的 NFT 项目。 但是，实现此克隆功能对于开发人员而言可能既耗时又容易出错。

用户触发的部署 SDK 的优势

以下是使用 SDK 的一些好处：

简化开发过程

  • 降低出错风险
  • 加快上市时间
  • 实施SDK

安装

要使用 SDK，您首先需要使用以下命令从 CLI 安装它：

请注意，@bunzz/deploy-sdk 目前不支持 ethers 版本 6。

实施过程

完整的实施过程包括以下步骤：

  1. 从资源管理器中找到合适的智能合约模块。

  2. 如果您找不到合适的模块，请从存储库页面上传您自己的智能合约模块（需要注册）。

  3. 从模块模板页面复制模块模板 ID。

  1. 使用SDK实现deploy函数，如下图：
 import { deploy } from '@bunzz/deploy-sdk'; import { providers } from 'ethers'; const TEMPLATE_ID = '<TEMPLATE_ID>'; const cloneContract = async () => { // get a Signer object in the ethers' style. const signer = getSigner(); // Arguments for the constructor as an array. // The types must follow the ethers style. const arg = ['dummy', 123456]; // The return value is the same as the ethers one. const tx = await deploy(TEMPLATE_ID, signer, args); // You can get receipt as well. const receipt = await tx.wait(); }

使用自动 DAO 创建平台的示例，在没有用户触发的部署 SDK 和使用 SDK 的情况下，过程中的差异将是显着的。 借助 SDK，开发人员可以从头开始创建自定义解决方案。 借助 SDK，他们可以用最少的编码轻松集成克隆功能。

通过提供 User Triggered Deployment SDK 等工具，我们旨在让开发人员更轻松地创建满足用户需求的 DApp。

结论

按照上面概述的安装和实施过程，开发人员可以轻松地将 SDK 集成到他们的 DApp 中并改善用户体验。

如果您有任何问题或反馈，请随时通过Discord联系我们。 立即开始使用Bunzz构建您的 DApp！



原文:

Decentralized applications (DApps) have the potential to transform the way we interact with technology and conduct transactions. However, building and deploying smart contracts on a blockchain can be time-consuming and challenging for developers.

The Bunzz User Triggered Deployment SDK aims to simplify this process by providing developers with new functionality to implement contract deployment and cloning features more easily, improving the user experience for decentralized applications (DApps).

This article will discuss the contract cloning problem, the user Triggered Deployment SDK benefits, and how to use it.

The Problem with Contract Cloning

Sometimes, DApps require users to deploy or clone the same contract multiple times. For example, no-code web3 tools like DAO platforms, protocols like Uniswap, and NFT marketplaces allow users to create their own NFT projects. However, implementing this cloning functionality can be time-consuming and error-prone for developers.

The Benefits of the User-Triggered Deployment SDK

Bunzz’s User Triggered Deployment SDK solves this problem, allowing developers to quickly and easily integrate the cloning functionality into their DApps. Here are some of the benefits of using the SDK:

Simplifies the development process

  • Reduces the risk of errors
  • Speeds up time to market
  • Implementing the SDK

Installation

To use the SDK, you first need to install it from your CLI using the following command:

npm install @bunzz/deploy-sdk ethers@5.7.0

Note that @bunzz/deploy-sdk does not currently support ethers version 6.

Implementation Process

The complete implementation process includes the following steps:

  1. Find an appropriate smart contract module from the explorer.

  2. If you can’t find a suitable module, upload your own smart contract module from the repository page (requires signing up).

  3. Copy the module template ID from the module template page.

  1. Implement the deploy function using the SDK as shown below: 
import { deploy } from '@bunzz/deploy-sdk';
import { providers } from 'ethers';

const TEMPLATE_ID = '<TEMPLATE_ID>';

const cloneContract = async () => {

  // get a Signer object in the ethers' style.
  const signer = getSigner();

  // Arguments for the constructor as an array.
  // The types must follow the ethers style.
  const arg = ['dummy', 123456];

  // The return value is the same as the ethers one.
  const tx = await deploy(TEMPLATE_ID, signer, args);

  // You can get receipt as well.
  const receipt = await tx.wait();
}

Using the example of an auto DAO creation platform, the difference in the process would be significant without the User Triggered Deployment SDK and with the SDK. With the SDK, developers can create a custom solution from scratch. With the SDK, they can easily integrate the cloning functionality with minimal coding.

Bunzz is a platform that helps to increase the number of blockchain developers who want to contribute to the Web3 scene. By providing tools like the User Triggered Deployment SDK, we aim to make it easier for developers to create DApps that meet users' needs.

Conclusion

Bunzz’s User Triggered Deployment SDK simplifies deploying and cloning smart contracts, reducing the risk of errors and speeding up time to market.

Following the installation and implementation process outlined above, developers can easily integrate the SDK into their DApps and improve the user experience.

If you have any questions or feedback, don't hesitate to contact us on Discord. Get started building your DApps with Bunzz now!
item.author: bunzzdev

item.title: 最简单的pyspark教程 | Simplest pyspark tutorial
item.description:
如果数据是新油，那么 Spark 就是新引擎。 近年来，我们见证了数据的指数级增长。 这是由于越来越多地使用实时收集数据的智能设备和传感器。 掌握如何使用此类数据的企业将在做出明智决策方面胜过同行。 要根据此类数据做出明智的决策，我们需要理解和处理这些数据。 当我们要处理庞大的数据集时，Spark 就派上用场了，因为它在数据处理过程中使用了并行集群。 我将介绍 Spark 的一些基础知识。 在继续之前，设置您的环境并在您的计算机上安装 Pyspark。 我正在使用这个数据集
要创建 Spark 弹性分布式数据集 (RDD)，我们首先创建一个 SparkSession。 这些 APIS 包括。

  1. 流上下文
  2. 以下代码行将在本教程中创建我们的会话。
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession spark= SparkSession.builder.appName('Erick').getOrCreate() df_spark= spark.read.csv('salary.csv') df_spark.show()
    创建会话后，以下代码行加载我们的数据集，并将数据存储在名为 df_spark 的数据框中。 当我们执行 df_spark.show() 时，我们将得到以下输出

    默认情况下，Spark 将显示前 20 行。 但是，如输出所示，数据集的标题放置错误。 为了正确地写这个，我们必须将标题设置为 true。

    df_spark=spark.read.option('header','true').csv('salary.csv') df_spark.show()
    输出现在应该如下所示；

    现在可以正确显示标题。 我们可以通过运行来查看行数

    df_spark.head(5)

    在本例中，我已将行数指定为 5，但您可以指定为任意数字。 当你执行这个时，你的输出应该是这样的

    [Row(MMM-YY='1/1/2016', Emp_ID=1, Age=28, Gender='Male', City='C23', Education_Level='Master', Salary=57387), Row(MMM-YY='2/1/2016', Emp_ID=1, Age=28, Gender='Male', City='C23', Education_Level='Master', Salary=57387), Row(MMM-YY=None, Emp_ID=None, Age=None, Gender=None, City=None, Education_Level=None, Salary=None), Row(MMM-YY='11/1/2017', Emp_ID=2, Age=31, Gender='Male', City='C7', Education_Level='Master', Salary=67016), Row(MMM-YY='12/1/2017', Emp_ID=2, Age=31, Gender='Male', City='C7', Education_Level='Master', Salary=67016)]


    在此之前，我们应该在加载数据集时将 inferschema 设置为 True。 这是这样做的

当我们运行它时，我们应该能够看到以下格式的数据类型。

当您在设置 inferSchema = True 之前打印架构时，您的输出将如下所示

这是因为 Spark 默认情况下会将所有内容解释为字符串 本教程将介绍 Spark 中的选择和删除函数、重命名列、填充缺失值、过滤以及按 Spark 中的函数分组。

选择功能。

要从数据集或数据框中选择列，使用 Select()。 选择 Age、gender、City、Education_Level 和 Salary 时，我们可以使用以下语法。

df_spark.select(['Age','gender','City','Education_Level','Salary']).show()


当我们选择这些列时，我们的输出将如下所示，

有时我们可能需要在数据集中添加一列。 例如，我们可以在 5 年后添加一列年龄
df_spark.withColumn('age after 5 years',df_spark['age']+5).show()

该行的输出如下。

有时我们的列可能与我们的数据集无关。例如，我们在分析中可能不需要 Emp_ID。 因此我们可以将其删除为
df_spark.drop('Emp_ID').show()

如上所示，Emp_ID 已被删除。 我们还可能需要重命名特定的列，这可以按如下方式完成。

df_spark.withColumnRenamed('MMM-YY','date_employed').show()


这会将 MMM-YY 重命名为 date_employed。

我们还需要有处理数据集中缺失数据的方法。 我们可以删除具有缺失值的行或填充缺失值。我们可以决定删除所有具有缺失值的行，这是按如下方式完成的
df_spark.na.drop().show()

当我们查看它的输出时，我们注意到所有具有一些缺失值的行都已被删除。 输出将如下所示。

我们还可以使用阈值给出一些条件。 例如，我们可以决定删除具有两个以上缺失值的行。 这可以作为
df_spark.na.drop(how='any',thresh=2).show()

其输出如下所示。 有一个缺失值的行不必删除

我们还可以决定删除具有缺失值的特定行。 这是按如下方式完成的

df_spark.na.drop(how="any",subset=['city']).show()


在这里，我们将删除所有具有缺失值的城市列行。 输出如下

我们还可以决定填充缺失值而不是删除它们。 这可以按如下方式完成
df_spark.na.fill('missing value',['age','salary']).show()
这里年龄和薪水列中的所有缺失值将被填充（缺失值）。 输出如下所示。

此类的输入列应该是数字，因为该类当前不支持分类特征。 这个类的使用如下图
from pyspark.ml.feature import Imputer

imputer = Imputer( inputCols = ['Age', 'Salary'], outputCols = ["{}_imputed".format(a) for a in ['Age ', 'salary']] ).setStrategy("median")
imputer.fit(df_spark).transform(df_spark).show()

其输出将添加 Age_imputed 和 Salary_imputed 列，如下所示。

我们还可能需要过滤我们的结果。 例如，我们可以将等于或小于 30 岁的年龄过滤为

df_spark.filter("Age <= 30").show()

如所见，显示的年龄低于 31 岁

我们还可以选择要过滤的列

df_spark.filter("Age <= 30").select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()


输出变为

我们可能还需要使用多个条件语句。 例如我们可能需要同时过滤薪水和年龄

df_spark.filter((df_spark['Age']<=30)& (df_spark['Salary']>= 170000)).select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()
输出变为

我们可以使用不比较语句作为

df_spark.filter(~((df_spark['Age']<=30)& (df_spark['Salary']>=170000))) .select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()


输出变为

我们还可能需要将数据分组到不同的列中。 例如，我们可以将 salary 与 education_level 和 gender 分组为

df_spark.groupBy('Education_Level','Gender').max('salary').show()


输出为

综上所述，pyspark 是 Apache spark 的 Python API，可帮助数据开发人员使用分布式计算框架对大型数据集进行数据处理任务。 它可以将列的一个或多个名称作为参数，并返回一个仅包含所选列的新数据框。 它将要删除的列作为参数，并返回一个没有删除列的新数据框。 我们在本教程中使用的另一个函数是 withColumnRenamed () 函数。 此函数用于重命名数据框中的特定列。 它首先接受两个参数，旧列的名称后跟新名称，它将返回重命名的列。 最后，我们查看了 groupBy() 函数，该函数用于按一列或多列对数据框中的数据进行分组。 它返回分组数据框，可用于[在分组数据中执行求和、计数或平均值等操作。

好消息……你可以在这里找到完整的笔记本（ drive.google.com/file/d/1Iy69g13tzCCksb ...）

你可以关注我 ( twitter.com/ErickNdurumo ) 或 ( www.linkedin.com/in/erick-muriungi-1500… )

编码快乐！！！

一旦被暂停，ndurumo254 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，ndurumo254 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ndurumo254 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ndurumo254 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ndurumo254 的方法：

降低 ndurumo254 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 ndurumo254 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

If data is the new oil, then Spark will be the new engine. In the recent few years, we have witnessed exponential growth in data. This is due to the increased use of smart devices and sensors that collect data in real-time. Businesses that will master how to use such data will outdo their counterparts in making intelligent decisions. To make an intelligent decision from such data, we need to understand and process it. Spark comes in handy when we want to process huge data sets because it uses parallel clusters during data processing. I am going to cover some basics of Spark. Spark supports different languages, and in this tutorial, I will be using Python. Before proceeding, set your environment and install Pyspark on your machine. I am using this dataset
(drive.google.com/file/d/1b2oL92aRU5_xLk…)
To create a spark resilient distributed dataset (RDD), we start by creating a SparkSession. Sparksession is an object that a programmer creates using a SparkSession builder pattern. Spark session is the first code in the Spark program. Spark session includes all the available Apis in different contexts. These APIS incldes.

  1. SparkContext
  2. StreamingContext
  3. SQLContext
  4. HiveContext

The following line of code will create our session in this tutorial.
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession spark= SparkSession.builder.appName('Erick').getOrCreate() df_spark= spark.read.csv('salary.csv') df_spark.show()
SparkSession.builder() returns a builder class with methods such as master(), appName(),and getOrCreate(). After creating the session the following lines of code loads our data sets and the data is stored in the data frame named df_spark. When we execute df_spark.show() we will get the following output

By default, the Spark will display the first 20 rows. However, as seen in the output, the header of the dataset is wrongly placed. To write this correctly, we have to set the header to true.

df_spark=spark.read.option('header','true').csv('salary.csv') df_spark.show()
The output should now be as follows;

The headers are now correctly shown. We can see the number of rows by running

df_spark.head(5)

In this case, I have specified the number of rows to be 5, but you can specify to any number. When you execute this, your output should look like

[Row(MMM-YY='1/1/2016', Emp_ID=1, Age=28, Gender='Male', City='C23', Education_Level='Master', Salary=57387), Row(MMM-YY='2/1/2016', Emp_ID=1, Age=28, Gender='Male', City='C23', Education_Level='Master', Salary=57387), Row(MMM-YY=None, Emp_ID=None, Age=None, Gender=None, City=None, Education_Level=None, Salary=None), Row(MMM-YY='11/1/2017', Emp_ID=2, Age=31, Gender='Male', City='C7', Education_Level='Master', Salary=67016), Row(MMM-YY='12/1/2017', Emp_ID=2, Age=31, Gender='Male', City='C7', Education_Level='Master', Salary=67016)]


Pyspark enables us to see the datatypes of each column by executing the printschema() method. Before this, we should set inferschema to True when loading the dataset. This is done as

df_spark=spark.read.option('header','true').csv('salary.csv', inferSchema=True) Then we should check the datatype of schema by

df_spark.printSchema()

When we run this, we should be able to see the datatypes in the following format.

When you printschema before setting inferSchema = True, your output will be as follows

This is because Spark, by default, will interpret everything as a string This tutorial will cover select and drop functions in Spark, renaming columns, filling the missing values, filtering, and grouping by functions in Spark.

Select function.

To select the column from the dataset or data frame Select() is used. When selecting Age, gender, City, Education_Level, and Salary, we can use the following syntax.

df_spark.select(['Age','gender','City','Education_Level','Salary']).show()


When we select these columns, our output will be as follows,

Sometimes we might need to add a column in the dataset. For example, we can add a column for age after 5years
df_spark.withColumn('age after 5 years',df_spark['age']+5).show()

The output of this line is as follows.

At times we can have columns that are irrelevant to our datasets.for example we might not be in need of Emp_ID in our analysis. Therefore we can drop it as
df_spark.drop('Emp_ID').show()

As seen above, the Emp_ID has been deleted. We can also need to rename specific columns, which can be done as follows.

df_spark.withColumnRenamed('MMM-YY','date_employed').show()


This will rename MMM-YY to date_employed.

We also need to have means of handling the missing data in the dataset. We can either drop the rows with missing values or fill in the missing values.we can decide to drop all the rows with the missing values and this is done as follows
df_spark.na.drop().show()

When we look at the output of this, we notice that all rows with some missing values have been deleted. The output will be as shown below.

We can also give some conditions using a threshold. For example, we can decide to delete rows with more than two missing values. This can be done as
df_spark.na.drop(how='any',thresh=2).show()

The output of this is as shown below. Rows with one missing value have not to be deleted

We can also decide to delete specific rows with missing values. This is done as follows

df_spark.na.drop(how="any",subset=['city']).show()


Here we will delete all city column rows with missing values. The output is as follows

We can also decide to fill in the missing values rather than deleting them. This can be done as follows
df_spark.na.fill('missing value',['age','salary']).show()
Here all the missing values in the age and salary columns will be filled with (missing value). The output is as shown below.

Pyspark has a class pyspark.ml.feature .Imputer that is used for completing missing values using mean, mode or median of the column that has a missing value. The input column of this class should be numeric because the class is not currently supporting categorical features. This class is used as shown below
from pyspark.ml.feature import Imputer

imputer = Imputer( inputCols = ['Age', 'Salary'], outputCols = ["{}_imputed".format(a) for a in ['Age ', 'salary']] ).setStrategy("median")
imputer.fit(df_spark).transform(df_spark).show()

The output of this will add Age_imputed and Salary_imputed columns, as shown below.

We can also need to filter our results. For example, we can filter ages equal to or below 30 years as

df_spark.filter("Age <= 30").show()

The output of this becomes as seen the displayed ages are below 31 years

We can also select columns to be filtered as

df_spark.filter("Age <= 30").select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()


The output becomes

We might also need to use more than one condition statement. Such as we can need to filter both salary and age as

df_spark.filter((df_spark['Age']<=30)& (df_spark['Salary']>= 170000)).select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()
The output becomes

We can use not comparison statement as

df_spark.filter(~((df_spark['Age']<=30)& (df_spark['Salary']>=170000))) .select(['Gender','Education_Level','salary']).show()


The output becomes

We can also need to group our data into various columns. For example, we can group salary with education_level and gender as

df_spark.groupBy('Education_Level','Gender').max('salary').show()


The output is as

In summary, pyspark is the Python API for Apache spark that helps data developers to carry out data processing tasks on large datasets using distributed computing framework. The select () function is used in the selection of a specific column from a data frame. It can take one or multiple names of the column as an argument, and it returns a new data frame with only the selected column. The drop () function is used to delete one or more columns from the data frames. It takes the columns to be deleted as its arguments and returns a new dataframe without the deleted columns. Another function we have used in this tutorial is a withColumnRenamed () function. This function is used to rename a specific column in the data frame. It takes two arguments first, the name of the old column followed by the new name and it will return the renamed column. Finally, we looked at the groupBy() function that is used to group data in the data frame by one or more columns. It returns grouped data frame that can be used to [perform operations such as sum, count,or mean in the grouped data.

Good news.. you can find full notebook here (drive.google.com/file/d/1Iy69g13tzCCksb…)

You can follow me (twitter.com/ErickNdurumo) or (www.linkedin.com/in/erick-muriungi-1500…)

Happy coding!!!

Aldi Fianda Putra - Feb 27
